`yaml title="monitoring/opensearch/helm/opensearch-dashboards-values.yaml"
opensearchHosts: https://opensearch-cluster-master:9200
replicaCount: 1

config:
 # /usr/share/opensearch-dashboards/config/opensearch_dashboards.yml
 # https://github.com/opensearch-project/OpenSearch-Dashboards/blob/main/config/opensearch_dashboards.yml
 opensearch_dashboards.yml: |-
 server.name: dashboards
 server.host: 0.0.0.0

 opensearch.hosts: ["https://opensearch-cluster-master:9200"]
 opensearch.username: kibanaserver

 # browser dashboard

 opensearch_security.cookie.secure: false

 opensearch.requestHeadersWhitelist: ["securitytenant", "authorization"]
 opensearch_security.multitenancy.enabled: true
 opensearch_security.multitenancy.tenants.enable_global: true
 opensearch_security.multitenancy.tenants.enable_private: true
 opensearch_security.multitenancy.tenants.preferred: ["Private", "Global"]
 opensearch_security.multitenancy.enable_filter: false

 # н…ҢмҠӨнҠён•  л•Ң true
 logging.verbose: false

opensearchAccount:
 secret: "opensearch-dashboards-account"
 keyPassphrase:
 enabled: false

resources:
 requests:
 cpu: 200m
 memory: 1Gi
 limits:
 memory: 2Gi
`

`shell
helm upgrade -i opensearch-dashboards opensearch/opensearch-dashboards \

`

### лӢӨмӨ‘ мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ м§Җмӣҗ(Multi-user support)

- https://github.com/aws/aws-parallelcluster/wiki/Multi-User-Support

EFS, FSx л“ұ м»ҙн“ЁнҠё л…ёл“ңк°Җ м ‘к·ј к°ҖлҠҘн•ң кІҪлЎңлҘј /sharedлқјкі  к°Җм •н–Ҳмқ„ л•Ң, м•„лһҳ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј нҷңмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ /shared/userlistfileм—җ мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ лӘ©лЎқмқ„ мһ‘м„ұн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
echo "$USER,id -u $USER" >> /shared/userlistfile
`

`shell title="/shared/userlistfile"
hhk7734,1001
`

compute-node-on-node-configured.sh мҠӨнҒ¬лҰҪнҠём—җ м•„лһҳ лӮҙмҡ©мқ„ м¶”к°Җн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell title="compute-node-on-node-configured.sh"
#!/bin/bash

. "/etc/parallelcluster/cfnconfig"

IFS=","

if [ "${cfn_node_type}" = "ComputeFleet" ]; then
 while read USERNAME USERID
 do
 # -M do not create home since head node is exporting /homes via NFS
 # -u to set UID to match what is set on the head node
 if ! [ $(id -u $USERNAME 2>/dev/null || echo -1) -ge 0 ]; then
 useradd -M -u $USERID $USERNAME
 fi
 done < "/shared/userlistfile"
fi
``

title: Podman м„Өм№ҳ л°Ҹ нҷҳкІҪ м„Өм •
text:
Podman к°ңмҡ” л¬ём„ңм—җм„ң к°Ғ лҸ„кө¬мқҳ м—ӯн• мқ„ лЁјм Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

- Podman Installation Instructions
- buildah Installation Instructions

## м„Өм№ҳ

``shell
sudo pacman -S podman buildah skopeo
`

`shell
yay -S podman-desktop
`

`shell
sudo apt install podman buildah skopeo
`

`shell
flatpak install flathub io.podman_desktop.PodmanDesktop
`

## м„Өм •

### registries.conf

- /etc/containers/registries.conf
- /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/*.conf
- $HOME/.config/containers/registries.conf

`toml
unqualified-search-registries = ['docker.io', 'quay.io', 'registry.fedoraproject.org']
`

`toml
[[registry]]
location = ""
insecure = true
`

`toml
[[registry]]
prefix = ""
location = ""
`

prefixм—җ лҢҖн•ң мҡ”мІӯмқ„ locationмңјлЎң л°”кҝ”м„ң мІҳлҰ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`toml
[[registry]]
location = ""

[[registry.mirror]]
location = ""

[[registry.mirror]]
location = ""
insecure = true
`

м„ м–ёлҗң мҲңм„ңлЎң mirrorлҘј нҷ•мқён•ҳкі  л§Ҳм§Җл§үм—җ мһҗкё° мһҗмӢ мқ„ нҷ•мқён•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
podman info --format json | jq '.registries'
`

`shell
podman pull --log-level debug
`

debug лЎңк·ёлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©ҙ мӢӨм ңлЎң м–ҙл–Ө registryмҷҖ mirrorлҘј мЎ°нҡҢн•ҳлҠ”м§Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## мң м Җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ л§Өн•‘

- User Namespace

/etc/subuidмҷҖ /etc/subgid нҢҢмқјмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳм—¬ мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨм—җм„ң мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗк°Җ мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” uidмҷҖ gidлҘј кІ°м •н•ҳлҠ” кё°лҠҘмқ„ нҸ¬н•Ён•ң Linux л°°нҸ¬нҢҗм—җм„ңлҠ” useradd лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј нҶөн•ҙ мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗлҘј м¶”к°Җн–Ҳмқ„ л•Ң мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң 65536к°ңмқҳ uidмҷҖ gidлҘј н• лӢ№н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell title="/etc/subuid, /etc/subgid"
hhk7734:100000:65536
`

`shell
sudo useradd test
`

`shell title="/etc/subuid, /etc/subgid"
hhk7734:100000:65536
test:165536:65536
`

LDAP л“ұмқҳ мӮ¬мҡ©мңјлЎң мқҙлҹ¬н•ң л§Өн•‘мқҙ м—ҶлӢӨл©ҙ, м§Ғм ‘ м¶”к°Җн•ҙм•јн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
sudo usermod \
 --add-subuids 100000-165535 \
 --add-subgids 100000-165535 \
 test
`

podmanмңјлЎң м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„ҲлҘј мӢӨн–үн–Ҳмқ„ л•Ң, м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ лӮҙл¶Җм—җм„ң мҷёл¶Җ мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗмҷҖ лӮҙл¶Җ мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗк°Җ м–ҙл–»кІҢ л§Өн•‘лҗҳм–ҙ мһҲлҠ”м§Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
podman run alpine cat /proc/self/uid_map
 0 1000 1
 1 100000 65536
`

| л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ м•Ҳ uid | л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ л°– uid | кёёмқҙ |
| ------------------- | ------------------- | ----- |
| 0 | 1000 | 1 |
| 1 | 100000 | 65536 |

мқҙлҘј н•ҙм„қн•ҳл©ҙ лӮҙл¶Җ uid 0мқҖ мҷёл¶Җ uid 1000м—җ л§Өн•‘лҗҳкі , лӮҙл¶Җ uid 1 ~ 65536мқҖ мҷёл¶Җ uid 100000 ~ 165535м—җ к°Ғк°Ғ л§Өн•‘лҗңлӢӨлҠ” кІғмқ„ м•Ң мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

podman мӮ¬мҡ© мӨ‘м—җ л§Өн•‘мқ„ м¶”к°Җн•ңлӢӨл©ҙ м•„лһҳ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҙм•ј н•©лӢҲлӢӨ. мӢӨн–ү мӨ‘мқё м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„ҲлҠ” мӨ‘лӢЁлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
podman system migrate
`

- https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/rootless-podman-nfs

NFS н”„лЎңнҶ мҪңм—җлҠ” мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ к°ңл…җмқҙ м—ҶмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. rootless podmanмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•  л•Ң кё°ліё graphrootлҠ” $HOME/.local/share/containers/storageмқёлҚ°, NFSлЎң нҷҲ л””л үнҶ лҰ¬лҘј кіөмң н•ҳкі  мһҲлӢӨл©ҙ л¬ём ңк°Җ л°ңмғқн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`ini title="~/.config/containers/storage.conf"
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/user/2081"
graphroot = "/var/tmp/hyeonki/containers/storage"
``

мң„мҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ м„Өм •мқ„ м¶”к°Җн•ҙм„ң NFSк°Җ м•„лӢҢ лЎңм»¬ л””мҠӨнҒ¬м—җ м ҖмһҘн•ҳлҸ„лЎқ м„Өм •н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
snippet:
вҖҰl flathub io.podman_desktop.PodmanDesktop
`

- /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/*.conf
- $HOME/.config/conвҖҰ

title: Podman мӮ¬мҡ© к°Җмқҙл“ң
text:
Podman к°ңмҡ” л¬ём„ңмҷҖ м„Өм№ҳ л°Ҹ нҷҳкІҪ м„Өм • л¬ём„ңлҘј лЁјм Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## мқҙлҜём§Җ л№Ңл“ң

### Dockerfile

``shell
buildah bud [] []
`

- flags
 - -f|--file : Dockerfile кІҪлЎң
 - -t|--tag

### ShellScript

`shell title="containerize.sh"
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -x

build=$(buildah from golang:1.17)
build_root=$(buildah mount $build)

buildah copy $build . /app
buildah config --workingdir /app $build
buildah run $build go get -u github.com/gin-gonic/gin
buildah run $build env CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -a -ldflags '-s' -o app

runtime=$(buildah from scratch)

buildah copy $runtime $build_root/app/app /usr/bin/
buildah config --cmd "app" $runtime
buildah commit $runtime ping:1.0.0

buildah rm $runtime

buildah unmount $build_root
buildah rm $build
`

`shell
buildah unshare sh containerize.sh
`

## мқҙлҜём§Җ кҙҖлҰ¬

### л Ҳм§ҖмҠӨнҠёлҰ¬м—җ лЎңк·ёмқё

`shell
skopeo login []
`

`shell
aws ecr get-login-password --region | skopeo login --username AWS --password-stdin .dkr.ecr..amazonaws.com
`

`shell
aws ecr-public get-login-password --region | skopeo login --username AWS --password-stdin public.ecr.aws/
`

### мқҙлҜём§Җ мӮӯм ң

`shell
podman image rm [] [ ...]
`

- -a|--all
- -f|--force

### мқҙлҜём§Җ мҳ®кё°кё°

`shell
skopeo copy []
`

- flags
 - --dest-creds=:
 - --dest-tls-verify=treu
- image
 - containers-storage:localhost/[:tag]
 - docker:///[:tag]

### inspect

`shell
skopeo inspect []
`

### нҢҢмқјлЎң м ҖмһҘн•ҳкі  л¶Ҳлҹ¬мҳӨкё°

`shell
podman save -o .tar
`

`shell
podman load -i .tar
`

## м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ кҙҖлҰ¬

### м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ мӢӨн–үн•ҳкё°

`shell
podman run [] [ [ ...]]
`

мғҲлЎңмҡҙ м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳм—җ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӢӨн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

-
 - --cpus : CPU limit
 - -d|--detach: м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„ҲлҘј л°ұк·ёлқјмҡҙл“ңм—җм„ң мӢӨн–үн•ҳкі  м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ IDлҘј м¶ңл Ҙн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - --entrypoint |'["", "", ..."]': Dockerfileмқҳ ENTRYPOINTм—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - --env-file : .envмІҳлҹј нҷҳкІҪ ліҖмҲҳ к°’мқҙ мһҲлҠ” нҢҢмқјлЎң нҷҳкІҪ ліҖмҲҳлҘј м„Өм •н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - -e|--env [=]: нҷҳкІҪ ліҖмҲҳлҘј м„Өм •н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - л§Ң мһҲлҠ” кІҪмҡ° нҳёмҠӨнҠём—җм„ң н•ҙлӢ№ нҷҳкІҪ ліҖмҲҳлҘј м°ҫм•„ м„Өм •н•ҳкі  м—Ҷмңјл©ҙ м„Өм •н•ҳм§Җ м•ҠмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. * globлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - -it: STDINмқ„ м—ҙкі  м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳм—җ к°ҖмғҒ н„°лҜёл„җмқ„ н• лӢ№н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - -m|--memory : л©”лӘЁлҰ¬ limit
 - : b, k, m, g
 - --name : м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ мқҙлҰ„
 - --network "host": нҳёмҠӨнҠё л„ӨнҠёмӣҢнҒ¬ мӮ¬мҡ©
 - -p|--publish :[/]: нҸ¬нҠё нҸ¬мӣҢл”©
 - --rm: мў…лЈҢ мӢң м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ мӮӯм ң
 - -v|--volume :[:]: нҳёмҠӨнҠё л””л үн„°лҰ¬лҘј м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ л””л үн„°лҰ¬м—җ л§ҲмҡҙнҠён•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 -
 - ro: мқҪкё° м „мҡ©
 - rw: мқҪкё° м“°кё°
 - z: кіөмң  ліјлҘЁмңјлЎң м„Өм •н•ҳм—¬ лӘЁл“  м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳк°Җ мқҪкі  м“ё мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- [ [ ...]]
 - Dockerfileмқҳ CMDм—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

### м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ лҰ¬мҠӨнҠё

`shell
podman ps []
`

- -a|--all: лӘЁл“  м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ м¶ңл Ҙ
- -l|--latest: л§Ҳм§Җл§ү м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ м¶ңл Ҙ
- -q|--quiet: м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ IDл§Ң м¶ңл Ҙ

### мӢӨн–ү мӨ‘ м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳм—җ лӘ…л №м–ҙ мӢӨн–ү

`shell
podman exec [] [arg ...]
`

мӢӨн–ү мӨ‘мқё м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳм—җ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӢӨн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

- flags
 - -it: STDINмқ„ м—ҙкі  м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳм—җ к°ҖмғҒ н„°лҜёл„җ н• лӢ№
- container
 - container ID лҳҗлҠ” мқҙлҰ„

### м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ мӮӯм ң

`shell
podman rm []
`

- -f|--force: running лҳҗлҠ” unstable мғҒнғңмқҳ м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„ҲлҸ„ мӮӯм ң
- -a|--all: лӘЁл“  м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ мӮӯм ң

### м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ нҢҢмқј м „мҶЎ

`shell
podman cp [] [:] [:]
`

нҳёмҠӨнҠёмҷҖ м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ лҳҗлҠ” м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„ҲмҷҖ м»Ён…Ңмқҙл„Ҳ мӮ¬мқҙм—җм„ң нҢҢмқјмқ„ ліөмӮ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

## Kubernetesм—җм„ң мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳкё°

`yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
 name: podman
spec:

 - name: main
 image: quay.io/podman/stable
 args:
 - sleep
 - infinity
 securityContext:
 privileged: true
``

вҖҰ flags

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -x

build=$(buildah from golang:1.17)

title: Prometheus Operator Alertmanager CR мғқм„ұн•ҳкё°
text:
## Alertmanager

- Prometheus Operator / API reference # Alertmanager

``yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Alertmanager
metadata:
 name:
 namespace:
spec:
 affinity: {}
 storage:
 volumeClaimTemplate:
 spec:
 storageClassName: ceph-block
 accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
 resources:
 requests:
 storage: 5Gi
`

Alertmanager CRмқ„ мғқм„ұн•ҳкі  ServiceлҘј нҷ•мқё(лҳҗлҠ” мғқм„ұ)н•ң нӣ„, мқҙлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•  Prometheusм—җ кҙҖл Ё м •ліҙлҘј м„Өм •н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

### AlertmanagerConfig мҲҳм§‘н•ҳкё°

`yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Alertmanager
spec:
 alertmanagerConfigSelector: {}
`

- alertmanagerConfigNamespaceSelector
 - LabelSelector
 - nullлЎң м„Өм •н•ҳл©ҙ Alertmanagerк°Җ м„ м–ёлҗң Namespaceм—җм„ңл§Ң AlertmanagerConfigлҘј м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- alertmanagerConfigSelector
 - LabelSelector

## AlertmanagerConfig

- Prometheus Operator / API reference # AlertmanagerConfig

`yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1beta1
kind: AlertmanagerConfig
metadata:
 name:
 namespace:
spec:
 route:
 receiver:

 - name:
 : []
`

- receivers: []

 - slackConfigs: []`
 - Prometheus / Configuration # slack_config

## Alertmanager

- Prometheus Operator / API reference # Alertmanager

``yaml
apiVersion: monitвҖҰ

title: prometheus-operator
text:
## Kubernetes нҳёнҷҳм„ұ

- https://github.com/prometheus-operator/kube-prometheus#kubernetes-compatibility-matrix

## м„Өм№ҳ

`

&& helm search repo prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack -l | head -n 10

`

helm show values prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack \
 --version 77.11.1 \
kube-prometheus-stack-77.11.1.yaml
`

`yaml title="kube-prometheus-stack-values.yaml"
fullnameOverride: prometheus-stack

commonLabels: {}

defaultRules:
 # Chartк°Җ м ңкіөн•ҳлҠ” кё°ліё alert and record rulesмқҳ м§‘н•©

- prometheusOperator
 - enabled: true: -operatorлҘј м„Өм№ҳн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
helm template prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack \
 --version 77.11.1 \
 -n prometheus-stack \

kube-prometheus-stack.yaml
`

helm upgrade -i prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack \

`

## м—…к·ёл Ҳмқҙл“ң

- prometheus-community GitHub / kube-prometheus-stack / UPGRADE.md

## мӮӯм ң

`shell
helm uninstall -n prometheus-stack prometheus-operator
`

helmмңјлЎң мӮӯм ңн•ң нӣ„ CRDs мӮӯм ңлҘј мң„н•ҙ м•„лһҳ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҙмӨҚлӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
kubectl get crd -oname | grep --color=never 'monitoring.coreos.com' | xargs kubectl delete
``

title: ServiceMonitor/PodMonitor
text:
## ServiceMonitor

- Prometheus Operator / Docs / API reference # ServiceMonitor
- Prometheus / Docs / Configuration # scrape_config

ServiceMonitorлҠ” мҲҳм§‘н•ҳкі  мӢ¶мқҖ ServiceлҘј л“ұлЎқн•ҳлҠ” CRDмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. kubernetes_sd_configsк°Җ role: endpoints м„Өм •кіј н•Ёк»ҳ мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң м¶”к°Җлҗҳкі  кҙҖл Ё labelмқҙ м¶”к°Җлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

``yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
 labels:
 loliot.net/prometheus: monitoring
spec:
 jobLabel: istio
 selector:
 matchLabels:
 :
 namespaceSelector:
 any: true
 endpoints:
 - port: http-monitoring # Serviceм—җм„ң prometheusлҘј мң„н•ҙ л…ём¶ңн•ң port
 path: /metrics # кё°ліёк°’: /metrics
 interval: 15s
`

- jobLabel:
 - Service.metadata.labelsм—җ л§Өм№ӯлҗҳлҠ” labelKeyмқҳ к°’мқ„ jobмқҳ к°’мңјлЎң мӮ¬мҡ©н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - м„ м–ён•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡмңјл©ҙ Service.metadata.nameмқҳ к°’мқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

 - any:

 - any: false, matchNames: []лЎң м„Өм •н•ҳл©ҙ ServiceMonitor.metadata.namespaceм—җ м„ м–ёлҗң Serviceл§Ң лӘЁлӢҲн„°л§Ғ н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
- selector: {}
 - LabelSelector
- attachMetadata
 - л°ңкІ¬лҗң targetм—җ metadataлҘј м¶”к°Җн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - н•ҙлӢ№ metadataлҘј мҲҳм§‘н•ҳкё° мң„н•ң Roleмқҙ н•„мҡ”н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - node:
- endpoints: []
 - scheme: http
 - port:
 - path: /metrics
 - interval:
 - мҲҳм§‘ мЈјкё°мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - м„Өм •н•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡмңјл©ҙ global.scrape_intervalмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - scrapeTimeout:
 - мҲҳм§‘ мӢңк°„мқҙ м„Өм •лҗң мӢңк°„мқ„ мҙҲкіјн•ҳл©ҙ мҲҳм§‘мқ„ мӢӨнҢЁлЎң к°„мЈјн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - м„Өм •н•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡмңјл©ҙ global.scrape_timeoutмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң м¶”к°Җлҗң labelмқ„ м ңкұ°н•ҳл Өл©ҙ м•„лһҳ м„Өм •мқ„ м¶”к°Җн•ҳл©ҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

## PodMonitor

- Prometheus Operator / Docs / API reference # PodMonitor
- Prometheus / Docs / Configuration # scrape_config

PodMonitorлҠ” мҲҳм§‘н•ҳкі  мӢ¶мқҖ Podмқ„ л“ұлЎқн•ҳлҠ” CRDмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. Serviceк°Җ м—ҶлҠ” PodмқҙлӮҳ, Serviceк°Җ мһҲм§Җл§Ң к·ё мӨ‘ нҠ№м • Labelмқҙ мһҲлҠ” Podмқ„ лӘЁлӢҲн„°л§Ғн•  л•Ңл§Ң мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳкі , к·ё мҷём—җлҠ” ServiceMonitorлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳмӢңлҠ” кІғмқҙ мўӢмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: PodMonitor
metadata:
 name: envoy-stats
 namespace: monitoring
 labels:
 app: istio-proxy
spec:
 jobLabel: envoy-stats
 selector:
 matchExpressions: # labelsм—җ istio-prometheus-ignore к°Җ м—ҶлҠ” лӘЁл“  PodлҘј к°җмӢңн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - { key: istio-prometheus-ignore, operator: DoesNotExist }
 namespaceSelector:
 any: true
 podMetricsEndpoints:
 - path: /stats/prometheus
 interval: 15s
 relabelings:
 - action: keep
 sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_container_name]
 regex: "istio-proxy"
 - action: keep
 sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotationpresent_prometheus_io_scrape]
 - sourceLabels: [__address__, __meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_port]
 action: replace
 regex: ([^:]+)(?::\d+)?;(\d+)
 replacement: $1:$2
 targetLabel: __address__
 - action: labeldrop
 regex: "__meta_kubernetes_pod_label_(.+)"
 - sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
 action: replace
 targetLabel: namespace
 - sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_name]
 action: replace
 targetLabel: pod_name
`

### relabel_configs

- Prometheus / Docs / Configuration # relabel_config

мҠӨнҒ¬лһ©н•ҳкё° м „м—җ м„ңл№„мҠӨ л””мҠӨм»ӨлІ„лҰ¬ л“ұм—җ мқҳн•ҙ м„Өм •лҗң л Ҳмқҙлё”мқ„ мЎ°мһ‘н•ҳлҠ” м„Өм •мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. м„Өм •лҗң мҲңм„ңлЎң мӢӨн–үлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. __лЎң мӢңмһ‘н•ҳлҠ” л Ҳмқҙлё”мқҖ лҰ¬л Ҳмқҙлё”л§Ғ нӣ„ м ңкұ°лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`yaml
spec:
 endpoints:
 - relabelings:
 - sourceLabels:
 -
 separator: ";"
 action: replace # кё°ліёк°’: replace

 # modulus:

`

 - к°’мқҙ м—¬лҹ¬к°ңмқё кІҪмҡ° к°’мқ„ м—°кІ°н•ҳкё° мң„н•ң кө¬л¶„мһҗмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 -
- actions

 - keep: sourceLabelsмӨ‘ regexмҷҖ к°’мқҙ л§Өм№ӯлҗҳлҠ” л Ҳмқҙлё”л§Ң мң м§Җн•©лӢҲлӢӨ
 - drop: sourceLabelsмӨ‘ regexмҷҖ к°’мқҙ л§Өм№ӯлҗҳлҠ” л Ҳмқҙлё”мқ„ м ңкұ°н•©лӢҲлӢӨ
 - lowercase
 - uppercase
 - hashmod
 - labelmap
 - labeldrop
 - labelkeep

__лЎң мӢңмһ‘н•ҳлҠ” л Ҳмқҙлё”мқҖ relabel кіјм •мқҙ лӘЁл‘җ лҒқлӮң нӣ„м—җлҠ” м ңкұ°лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

## Prometheus

- Prometheus Operator / API reference # Prometheus

specм—җ л§һлҠ” prometheus StatefulSetsмқ„ мғқм„ұн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1

Scrape м„Өм • м¶”к°ҖлҘј м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ л°©лІ•мңјлЎң м§Ғм ‘ мһ‘м„ұн•ҙ л„Јмқ„ мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

title: Promtail Pipeline(лЎңк·ё ліҖнҷҳ)
text:
## Pipeline

- Pipeline

promtail мӢңмһ‘ мӢң discovery(or static) -\> relabel -\> pipeline мҲңмңјлЎң м§„н–үлҗҳл©°, pipelineмқҙ л¬ҙн•ң л°ҳліөлҗҳл©ҙм„ң лЎңк·ёлҘј мҲҳм§‘н•ҳкІҢ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

pipelineм—җлҠ” stageлқјлҠ” лӢЁкі„к°Җ мһҲкі , м„ м–ёлҗң мҲңм„ңлЎң мӢӨн–үлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. stageм—җм„ңлҠ” Parsing, Transform, Action, Filtering мһ‘м—…мқ„ мҲҳн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

pipelineм—җм„ң мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” лҚ°мқҙн„°лҠ” лӢӨмқҢкіј к°ҷмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

- Labels
 - discovery(or static) + relabel лӢЁкі„м—җм„ң мғқм„ұлҗң labels
 - м•һм„  stageм—җм„ң мғқм„ұлҗң labels
- Extracted Map
 - discovery(or static) + relabel лӢЁкі„м—җм„ң мғқм„ұлҗң labels
 - м•һм„  stageм—җм„ң мғқм„ұлҗң key-value map
- timestamp
- log line

``yaml
pipeline_stages:
 - :
 :
`

## Parsing stage

### cri

- Pipeline cri

`yaml
cri: {}
`

criлҠ” м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ мһ‘м—…мқ„ мҲҳн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`yaml
- regex:
 expression: "^(?s)(?P\\S+?) (?Pstdout|stderr) (?P\\S+?) (?P.*)$"
- labels:
 stream:
- timestamp:
 source: time
 format: RFC3339Nano
- output:
 source: content
`

### multiline

- Pipeline multiline

`yaml
multiline:
 # https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax
 firstline:

`

## Transform stage

### template

- Pipeline template
- stages/template.go

`yaml
template:

 # https://pkg.go.dev/text/template

`

 - Extracted Mapм—җ мһҲмңјл©ҙ н•ҙлӢ№ keyмқҳ valueлҘј н…ңн”Ңл ҲмқҙнҢ… лҗң к°’мңјлЎң лҢҖмІҙн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - Extracted Mapм—җ м—Ҷмңјл©ҙ н•ҙлӢ№ keyлҘј мғқм„ұн•ҳкі  н…ңн”Ңл ҲмқҙнҢ… лҗң к°’мңјлЎң лҢҖмІҙн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
-
 - ліҖмҲҳ
 - {{ .Value }}: Extracted Mapм—җм„ң source м—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•ҳлҠ” к°’мқ„ к°Җм ёмҳөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - {{ .Entry }}: лЎңк·ё лқјмқё м „мІҙлҘј к°Җм ёмҳөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - {{ . }}: Extracted Mapм—җм„ң м—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•ҳлҠ” к°’мқ„ к°Җм ёмҳөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## Pipeline

- Pipeline

promtail мӢңмһ‘ мӢң discovery(or static) -\> relabel -\> pipeline мҲңмңјлЎң м§„н–үлҗҳл©°, pipelineмқҙ л¬ҙн•ң л°ҳліөлҗҳл©ҙм„ңвҖҰ

title: Pulumi import/export
text:
## pulumi refresh

нҳ„мһ¬ мҠӨнғқмқҳ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ мғҒнғңлҘј мӢӨм ң нҒҙлқјмҡ°л“ңмҷҖ л№„көҗн•ҳм—¬ м—…лҚ°мқҙнҠё н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

``shell
pulumi refresh [flags]
`

## pulumi import

`shell
pulumi import [type] [name] [id] [flags]
`

- https://www.pulumi.com/registry/packages/aws/api-docs/

м§Ғм ‘ мғқм„ұн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ лӢӨлҘё н”„лЎңк·ёлһЁм—җ мқҳн•ҙ мғқм„ұлҗң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј нҳ„мһ¬ н”„лЎңм қнҠём—җ м¶”к°Җн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. м¶”к°Җлҗң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мҪ”л“ңлЎң м¶”к°Җн•  мҲҳ мһҲлҸ„лЎқ мҳҲмӢң мҪ”л“ңлҘј м¶ңл Ҙн•ҙмӨҚлӢҲлӢӨ.
мҷёл¶Җм—җм„ң к°Җм ёмҳӨ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҠ” кё°ліём ҒмңјлЎң н”„лЎңн…ҚнҢ… лҗҳм–ҙ мһҲм–ҙм„ң н”„лЎңн…ҚнҠёлҘј н’Җм–ҙмЈјм§Җ м•ҠлҠ” н•ң ліҖкІҪмқҙ м•Ҳлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell {1,44}
$ pulumi import aws:ec2/subnet:Subnet subnet-xxxxxxxxx

Previewing import (dev):
 Type Name Plan
 pulumi:pulumi:Stack master-dev
 = в””в”Җ aws:ec2:Subnet public_subnet import

Resources:
 = 1 to import
 xx unchanged

Do you want to perform this import? yes
Importing (dev):
 Type Name Status
 pulumi:pulumi:Stack master-dev
 = в””в”Җ aws:ec2:Subnet public_subnet imported

Outputs:
 ...

Resources:
 = 1 imported
 xx unchanged

Duration: 5s

Please copy the following code into your Pulumi application. Not doing so
will cause Pulumi to report that an update will happen on the next update command.

Please note that the imported resources are marked as protected. To destroy them
you will need to remove the protect option and run pulumi update *before*
the destroy will take effect.

public_subnet = aws.ec2.Subnet("public_subnet",
 assign_ipv6_address_on_creation=False,
 cidr_block="1.2.3.4/24",
 map_public_ip_on_launch=False,
 tags={
 "Name": "test",
 },
 vpc_id="vpc-xxxxx",
 opts=pulumi.ResourceOptions(protect=True))
`

## pulumi stack export

`shell
pulumi stack export [flags]
`

flags

- --file : нҢҢмқјлЎң м ҖмһҘ

## pulumi stack import

`shell
pulumi stack import [flags]
`

flags

- --file : нҢҢмқјмқ„ мқҪм–ҙмҳҙ

pulumi stack export --file stack.jsonмңјлЎң мҠӨнғқмқ„ м¶”м¶ңн•ҙм„ң мқҙлҰ„, id л“ұмқ„ ліҖкІҪн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ, лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј м¶”к°Җн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ мӮӯм ңн•ң нӣ„, pulumi stack import --file stack.json`мңјлЎң мҠӨнғқмқ„ лӢӨмӢң м„Өм •н•ҙ мӨ„ мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

title: Pulumi нҠңнҶ лҰ¬м–ј
text:
Pulumi к°ңмҡ” л¬ём„ңмҷҖ м„Өм№ҳ к°Җмқҙл“ң л¬ём„ңлҘј лЁјм Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## Tutorial

### Directory structure

``shell
test/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ __init__.py
в”ңв”Җв”Җ __main__.py
в”ңв”Җв”Җ pyproject.toml
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Pulumi.yaml
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Pulumi.test.dev.yaml
в””в”Җв”Җ src/
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ __init__.py
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ variable.py
 в””в”Җв”Җ ec2/
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ __init__.py
 в””в”Җв”Җ ec2.py
`

`shell
test/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ package.json
в”ңв”Җв”Җ tsconfig.json
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Pulumi.yaml
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Pulumi.test.dev.yaml
в””в”Җв”Җ src/
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ index.ts
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ variable.ts
 в””в”Җв”Җ ec2/
 в”ңв”Җв”Җ index.ts
 в””в”Җв”Җ ec2.ts
`

### Project file(Pulumi.yaml)

- https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/pulumi-yaml/

`yaml title="Pulumi.yaml"
name:
runtime:
 name: python
backend:
 url: s3://[?region=&profile=]
`

`yaml title="Pulumi.yaml"
name:
runtime: nodejs
backend:

`

### Package file

`toml title="pyproject.toml"
[tool.poetry]
name = ""
version = "0.1.0"
description = ""
authors = ["Hyeonki Hong "]

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
python = "^3.11"
pulumi = "^3.45.0"
pulumi-aws = "^5.19.0"

[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies]
black = "^22.10.0"
isort = "^5.10.1"
pylint = "^2.15.5"

[build-system]
build-backend = "poetry.core.masonry.api"
requires = ["poetry-core"]
`

`json title="package.json"
{
 "name": "",
 "main": "src/index.ts",
 "devDependencies": {
 "@types/node": "^14"
 },
 "dependencies": {
 "@pulumi/aws": "^5.19.0",
 "@pulumi/pulumi": "^3.45.0",
 "tsconfig-paths": "^4.1.0"
 }
}
`

`json title="tsconfig.json"
{
 "compilerOptions": {
 "strict": true,
 "outDir": "bin",
 "target": "es2016",
 "module": "commonjs",
 "moduleResolution": "node",
 "sourceMap": true,
 "experimentalDecorators": true,
 "pretty": true,
 "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
 "noImplicitReturns": true,
 "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
 "baseUrl": "./src",
 "paths": {
 "@src/*": ["./*"]
 }
 },
 "files": ["src/index.ts"]
}
`

### Stack Configuration file

- https://www.pulumi.com/registry/packages/aws/installation-configuration/#configuration-options

`shell
pulumi stack init test.dev
`

`yaml title="Pulumi.test.dev.yaml"
encryptionsalt: "*****"
config:
 # pulumi.Config("aws")
 aws:region:
 aws:profile:
`

### IAC code

`python title="src/variable.py"

stack_name = pulumi.get_stack()

config = pulumi.Config()
`

`python title="src/ec2/ec2.py"

from .. import variable

def create_ec2(name: str) -> aws.ec2.Instace:
 return aws.ec2.Instance(
 name,

 )

aws_instance = create_ec2("app-server")
`

from . import ec2

pulumi.export("instance_id", ec2.aws_instance.id)
pulumi.export("instance_public_ip", ec2.aws_instance.public_ip)
`

`python title="__main__.py"
from src import ec2
`

`ts title="src/variable.ts"

// const config = new pulumi.Config();
`

const appServerName = "app-server";
new aws.ec2.Instance(appServerName, {

ec2_;

`

const tsConfig = loadConfig(".");
if (tsConfig.resultType === "failed") {

});

title: RabbitMQ
text:
## м„Өм№ҳ

``shell
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami \
&& helm repo update bitnami
`

`shell
helm search repo bitnami/rabbitmq-cluster-operator -l | head -n 10
`

`shell
helm show values bitnami/rabbitmq-cluster-operator \
 --version 2.6.6 \
values.yaml
`

`shell
helm upgrade -i rabbitmq-operator bitnami/rabbitmq-cluster-operator \
 --version 2.6.6 \
 -n workflow \
 -f values.yaml
`

## CRDs

### RabbitmqCluster

- https://www.rabbitmq.com/kubernetes/operator/using-operator.html

`yaml
apiVersion: rabbitmq.com/v1beta1
kind: RabbitmqCluster
metadata:
 name: rabbitmq
 namespace: workflow
spec:
 replicas: 1
 rabbitmq:
 additionalConfig: |
 cluster_partition_handling = pause_minority
 vm_memory_high_watermark_paging_ratio = 0.99
 disk_free_limit.relative = 2.0
 collect_statistics_interval = 30000
 resources:
 requests:
 cpu: 200m
 memory: 2Gi
 limits:
 memory: 2Gi
 persistence:
 storage: 10Gi
 affinity:
 podAntiAffinity:
 requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
 - labelSelector:
 matchExpressions:
 - key: app.kubernetes.io/name
 operator: In
 values:
 - rabbitmq
 topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
 override:
 statefulSet:
 spec:
 template:
 spec:
 containers: []
 topologySpreadConstraints:
 - maxSkew: 1
 topologyKey: "topology.kubernetes.io/zone"
 whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule
 labelSelector:
 matchLabels:
 app.kubernetes.io/name: rabbitmq
`

### User && Permission

`yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
 name:
 namespace: workflow
type: Opaque
stringData:
 username:
 password:
`

- https://github.com/rabbitmq/messaging-topology-operator/blob/main/api/v1beta1/user_types.go

`yaml
apiVersion: rabbitmq.com/v1beta1
kind: User
metadata:
 name:
 namespace: workflow
spec:
 tags:
 - # management | policymaker | monitoring | administrator
 importCredentialsSecret:
 name:
 rabbitmqClusterReference:
 name: rabbitmq
 namespace: workflow
`

- https://github.com/rabbitmq/messaging-topology-operator/blob/main/api/v1beta1/permission_types.go

`yaml
apiVersion: rabbitmq.com/v1beta1
kind: Permission
metadata:
 name:
 namespace: workflow
spec:
 userReference:
 name:
 vhost: "/"
 permissions:
 configure: ".*"
 read: ".*"
 write: ".*"
 rabbitmqClusterReference:
 name: rabbitmq
 namespace: workflow
`

### Queue

- https://github.com/rabbitmq/messaging-topology-operator/blob/main/api/v1beta1/queue_types.go

`yaml
apiVersion: rabbitmq.com/v1beta1
kind: Queue
metadata:
 name:
 namespace: workflow
spec:
 name:
 type: quorum # classic | quorum | stream
 durable: true # falseл©ҙ serverк°Җ мһ¬мӢңмһ‘лҗ  л•Ң, нҒҗ лӮҙмҡ©мқҙ мӮӯм ңлҗЁ
 autoDelete: false # trueл©ҙ лӘЁл“  Subscriberк°Җ кө¬лҸ…мқ„ м·ЁмҶҢн•  л•Ң, нҒҗк°Җ мӮӯм ңлҗЁ
 rabbitmqClusterReference:
 name: rabbitmq
 namespace: workflow
`

Queue, Exchangeм—җм„ң arguments лҢҖмӢ  PolicyлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©ҙ мғқм„ұ нӣ„м—җлҸ„ мҳөм…ҳ ліҖкІҪмқ„ н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

### Exchange

- https://github.com/rabbitmq/messaging-topology-operator/blob/main/api/v1beta1/exchange_types.go

`yaml
apiVersion: rabbitmq.com/v1beta1
kind: Exchange
metadata:
 name:
 namespace: workflow
spec:
 name:
 type: direct # direct | fanout | headers | topic
 durable: true
 autoDelete: false
 rabbitmqClusterReference:
 name: rabbitmq
 namespace: workflow
`

### Binding

- https://github.com/rabbitmq/messaging-topology-operator/blob/main/api/v1beta1/binding_types.go

`yaml
apiVersion: rabbitmq.com/v1beta1
kind: Binding
metadata:
 name:
 namespace: workflow
spec:
 source: # Exchangeмқҳ мқҙлҰ„
 destination:
 destinationType: queue # queue | exchange
 # .мңјлЎң кө¬л¶„лҗҳлҠ” tagмқҳ лӮҳм—ҙ
 # *: мһ„мқҳмқҳ tag н•ҳлӮҳ
 # #: мһ„мқҳмқҳ tag 0 к°ң мқҙмғҒ
 # a.* -> a.a, a.b, ...
 # a.# -> a, a.a, a.a.a, ...
 routingKey:
 rabbitmqClusterReference:
 name: rabbitmq
 namespace: workflow
`

### Policy

- https://github.com/rabbitmq/messaging-topology-operator/blob/main/api/v1beta1/policy_types.go

`yaml
apiVersion: rabbitmq.com/v1beta1
kind: Policy
metadata:
 name:
 namespace: workflow
spec:
 name:
 vhost: /
 pattern: # мқҙ PolicyлҘј м Ғмҡ©н•  Queue лҳҗлҠ” Exchangeмқҳ мқҙлҰ„ нҢЁн„ҙ
 applyTo: all # queues | exchanges | all
 priority: 0
 definition:
 :
 rabbitmqClusterReference: # Policyк°Җ л“ұлЎқлҗ  нҒҙлҹ¬мҠӨн„°
 name: rabbitmq
 namespace: workflow
`

мӮ¬мҡ©лҗ  definitionмқҳ кІҪмҡ° management мӣ№м—җм„ң QueueлҳҗлҠ” Exchange`лҘј GUIлЎң мғқм„ұн•ҙліҙл©ҙ мүҪкІҢ м•Ң мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:

data:

apiVersion: apps/v1

metadata:
 name: redis

 app.kubernetes.io/name: redis
 app.kubernetes.io/version: "8.2.1"

 selector:

 app: redis
 app.kubernetes.io/name: redis
 template:
 metadata:
 labels:
 app: redis
 app.kubernetes.io/name: redis
 spec:
 containers:
 - name: redis
 image: docker.io/library/redis:8.2.1
 imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
 command:
 - redis-server
 args:
 - /usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf
 ports:
 - name: redis

 volumeMounts:
 - name: config

 - name: data
 mountPath: /data

 - name: config

title: s6-overlay м„Өм№ҳмҷҖ м„ңл№„мҠӨ м„Өм •
text:
s6-overlay к°ңмҡ” л¬ём„ңм—җм„ң н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨ кҙҖлҰ¬ кө¬мЎ°лҘј лЁјм Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

- s6-overlay GitHub

## м„Өм№ҳ

``dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ARG S6_OVERLAY_VERSION=3.2.1.0

# 2: Stop the container
ENV S6_BEHAVIOUR_IF_STAGE2_FAILS=2

RUN apt-get update \
 && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
 ca-certificates \
 curl \
 xz-utils \
 vim \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN curl -fsSLO https://github.com/just-containers/s6-overlay/releases/download/v${S6_OVERLAY_VERSION}/s6-overlay-noarch.tar.xz \
 && curl -fsSLO https://github.com/just-containers/s6-overlay/releases/download/v${S6_OVERLAY_VERSION}/s6-overlay-x86_64.tar.xz \
 && tar -C / -Jxpf s6-overlay-noarch.tar.xz \
 && tar -C / -Jxpf s6-overlay-x86_64.tar.xz \
 && rm s6-overlay-noarch.tar.xz \
 && rm s6-overlay-x86_64.tar.xz

ENTRYPOINT [ "/init" ]
`

## Create

``python

name = "ses"
ses = aws.ses.DomainIdentity(
 name,
 domain="",
 opts=pulumi.ResourceOptions(protect=True),
)

name = "ses-dkim"
ses_dkim = aws.ses.DomainDkim(
 name,
 domain="",
 opts=pulumi.ResourceOptions(parent=ses, protect=True),
)

for i in range(3):
 name = f"ses-dkim-record-{i}"
 cloudflare.Record(
 name,
 zone_id="",
 type="CNAME",
 name=ses_dkim.dkim_tokens[i].apply(
 lambda x: f"{x}._domainkey" # subdomainмқҙ мһҲлӢӨл©ҙ subdomainк№Ңм§Җ мһ…л Ҙ
 ),
 value=ses_dkim.dkim_tokens[i].apply(
 lambda x: f"{x}.dkim.amazonses.com"
 ),
 ttl=1, # auto
 opts=pulumi.ResourceOptions(parent=ses_dkim, protect=True),
 )
``

title: мЎҙмһ¬н•ҳлҠ” лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј TerraformмңјлЎң к°Җм ёмҳӨкё°

## лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ мЈјмҶҢ(Resource Address)

лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ мЈјмҶҢ()лҠ” лӘЁл“Ҳ мқҙлҰ„, лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ нғҖмһ…, лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ мқҙлҰ„ мңјлЎң кө¬м„ұлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. countлӮҳ for_each л“ұмқ„ мҚЁм„ң мғқм„ұн•ң лӘЁл“ҲмқҙлӮҳ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҠ” []лӮҳ [""]лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

``shell
[.[.]]
`

-
 - module.

-
 - .

 - .[""]

-
 - лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј нҠ№м •м§Җмқ„ мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” к°’мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp л¬ём„ңм—җм„ң к°Ғ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ м„ӨлӘ…м—җ importмӢң мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” idлҘј нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## import лё”лЎқмңјлЎң к°Җм ёмҳӨкё°

- https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import

`hcl

 to =
 id = ""
}

resource "" "" {
 # к°Җм ёмҳ¬ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨмқҳ м„Өм •мқ„ м ҒмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
}
`

лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ м„Өм •мқ„ л№ м§җм—Ҷмқҙ к°Җм ёмҳӨлҠ” кІғмқҙ м–ҙл Өмҡё мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. import лё”лЎқл§Ң м„ м–ён•ң нӣ„ -generate-config-out= мҳөм…ҳмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳм—¬ planмқ„ мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©ҙ к°Җм ёмҳ¬ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ м„Өм •мқ„ мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң мғқм„ұн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. м•„м§Ғ мӢӨн—ҳм Ғмқё кё°лҠҘмқҙлҜҖлЎң, н„°лҜёл„җм—җ м¶ңл Ҙлҗң лӮҙмҡ©кіј мғқм„ұлҗң нҢҢмқјмқ„ кјјкјјнһҲ нҷ•мқён•ҙм•ј н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf
`

лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ м„ м–ё нӣ„ terraform plan лӘ…л №мңјлЎң нҷ•мқён•ң нӣ„, terraform apply`лҘј н•ҳл©ҙ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨк°Җ мғҒнғң нҢҢмқјм—җ м¶”к°Җлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

title: л°ҳліөлҗҳлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҳ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј countлӮҳ for_eachлЎң мғқм„ұн•ҳкё°
description: л°ҳліөлҗҳлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҳ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј countлӮҳ for_eachлЎң мғқм„ұн•ҳкё°
text:
## count

- Terraform / Meta-Arguments / count

``hcl
resource "" "" {
 count =

 # ...
}
`

count.index ліҖмҲҳлҠ” л°ҳліөлҗҳлҠ” лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨмқҳ мқёлҚұмҠӨлҘј лӮҳнғҖлӮҙл©°, 0л¶Җн„° мӢңмһ‘н•©лӢҲлӢӨ. мҳҲлҘј л“Өм–ҙ, count = 3мңјлЎң м„Өм •н•ҳл©ҙ count.indexлҠ” мҲңм„ңм—җ л”°лқј 0, 1, 2мқҳ к°’мқ„ к°–мҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

countм—җ мқҳн•ҙ мғқм„ұлҗң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҠ” .[]лЎң м ‘к·јн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## for_each

- Terraform / Meta-Arguments / for_each

`hcl
resource "" "" {
 for_each =

 # ...
}
`

mapмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ° each.keyлҠ” л°ҳліөлҗҳлҠ” лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨм—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•ҳлҠ” mapмқҳ keyлҘј лӮҳнғҖлӮҙл©°, each.value ліҖмҲҳлҠ” mapмқҳ valueлҘј лӮҳнғҖлғ…лӢҲлӢӨ. мҳҲлҘј л“Өм–ҙ for_each = tomap({a = 1, b = 2 })лЎң м„Өм •н•ҳл©ҙ мҲңм„ңм—җ л”°лқј each.keyлҠ” aмҷҖ bмқҳ к°’мқ„ к°–кі , each.valueлҠ” 1кіј 2мқҳ к°’мқ„ к°–мҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

setмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ° each.keyмҷҖ each.valueлҠ” л°ҳліөлҗҳлҠ” лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨм—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•ҳлҠ” setмқҳ memberлҘј лӮҳнғҖлғ…лӢҲлӢӨ. мҳҲлҘј л“Өм–ҙ for_each = toset(["a", "b"])лЎң м„Өм •н•ҳл©ҙ each.keyмҷҖ each.valueлҠ” к°ҷмқҖ к°’мқ„ к°Җм§Җл©°, мҲңм„ңм—җ л”°лқј aмҷҖ bмқҳ к°’мқ„ к°–мҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

for_eachм—җ мқҳн•ҙ мғқм„ұлҗң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҠ” .[]`лЎң м ‘к·јн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

text:
## мӢӨм ң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨмҷҖ мғҒнғң лӘЁл‘җ мӮӯм ңн•ҳкё°

мӢӨм ң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨмҷҖ мғҒнғң лӘЁл‘җ мӮӯм ңн•ҳл Өл©ҙ мҪ”л“ңм—җм„ң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мӮӯм ңн•ҳкі  terraform applyлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©ҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

## мғҒнғңл§Ң мӮӯм ңн•ҳкё°

лӢӨлҘё нҲҙлЎң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ кҙҖлҰ¬лҘј мқҙм „н•ҳкұ°лӮҳ, лӢӨлҘё н”„лЎңм қнҠёлЎң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мқҙм „н•ҳлҠ” л“ұмқҳ мқҙмң лЎң мӢӨм ң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҠ” лӮЁкІЁл‘” мұ„лЎң мғҒнғңл§Ң мӮӯм ңн•ҙм•јн•ҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ°к°Җ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. мқҙ кІҪмҡ° м•„лһҳ лӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң мғҒнғңл§Ң мӮӯм ңн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

``shell
terraform state rm
`

- `
 - лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ мЈјмҶҢ(Resource Address)

мғҒнғңлҘј мӮӯм ңн•ң нӣ„, мҪ”л“ңм—җм„ңлҸ„ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мӮӯм ңн•ҙм•јн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
snippet:
## мӢӨм ң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨмҷҖ мғҒнғң лӘЁл‘җ мӮӯм ңн•ҳкё°

мӢӨм ң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨмҷҖ мғҒнғң лӘЁл‘җ мӮӯм ңн•ҳл Өл©ҙ мҪ”л“ңм—җм„ң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мӮӯм ңн•ҳкі  terraform applyлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©ҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

## мғҒнғңл§Ң мӮӯм ңн•ҳкё°

лӢӨлҘё нҲҙлЎң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ кҙҖлҰ¬лҘј мқҙм „н•ҳкұ°лӮҳ, лӢӨлҘё н”„лЎңм қнҠёлЎң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мқҙм „н•ҳлҠ” л“ұмқҳ мқҙмң лЎң мӢӨвҖҰ

title: Terraform Secret кҙҖлҰ¬н•ҳкё°
text:
## rsadecryptлҘј нҷңмҡ©н•ң Secret кҙҖлҰ¬

- rsadecrypt Function

opensslлӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң к°ңмқёнӮӨмҷҖ кіөк°ңнӮӨлҘј мғқм„ұн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. к°ңмқёнӮӨ мғқм„ұмӢң мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳлҠ” л№„нҠё мҲҳм—җ л”°лқј м•”нҳёнҷ”н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” л¬ёмһҗм—ҙмқҳ мөңлҢҖ кёёмқҙк°Җ лӢ¬лқјм§‘лӢҲлӢӨ.

``shell
openssl genrsa -out private.pem 4096
`

`shell
openssl rsa -in private.pem -pubout -out public.pem
`

м•„лһҳ лӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң мӣҗн•ҳлҠ” к°’мқ„ м•”нҳёнҷ”/ліөнҳёнҷ” н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
echo -n 'secret' \
 | openssl pkeyutl -encrypt -pubin -inkey public.pem \
 | base64 \
 | tr -d '\n' \
 | xargs -0 echo
`

`shell
echo -n 'encryptedSecret' \
 | base64 -d \
 | openssl pkeyutl -decrypt -inkey private.pem \
 | xargs -0 echo
`

м•”нҳёнҷ”лҗң к°’мқҖ Terraformм—җм„ң м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ л°©мӢқмңјлЎң мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`hcl
variable "private_pem_path" {
 type = string
}

locals {
 private_pem = file("${var.private_pem_path}")
}

output "secret" {
 value = rsadecrypt("encryptedSecret", local.private_pem)
}
``
snippet:
вҖҰ \
 | openssl pkeyutl -decrypt -inkey private.pem \
 | xargs -0 echo
`

м•”нҳёнҷ”лҗң к°’мқҖ Terraformм—җм„ң м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ л°©мӢқмңјлЎң мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

title: Terraform нҠңнҶ лҰ¬м–ј
text:
Terraform к°ңмҡ” л¬ём„ңмҷҖ м„Өм№ҳ к°Җмқҙл“ң л¬ём„ңлҘј лЁјм Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## Tutorial

- https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/files
 - TerraformмқҖ *.tf нҢҢмқјмқҙ мһҲлҠ” л””л үн„°лҰ¬лҘј н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ лӘЁл“ҲлЎң к°„мЈјн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - terraform лӘ…л №м–ҙлҠ” лі„лҸ„мқҳ м„ м–ё м—Ҷмқҙ лӘЁл“Ҳ лӮҙмқҳ лӘЁл“  *.tf нҢҢмқјмқ„ мӢӨн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - л””л үн„°лҰ¬ лӮҙмқҳ л””л үн„°лҰ¬ лҳҗлҠ” лӢӨлҘё мң„м№ҳмқҳ л””л үн„°лҰ¬лҠ” лі„лҸ„мқҳ лӘЁл“ҲлЎң к°„мЈјлҗҳл©°, лі„лҸ„мқҳ м„ м–ёмқҙ мһҲм–ҙм•јм§Җл§Ң мӢӨн–үлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - terraform лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӢӨн–үмӢңнӮӨлҠ” мң„м№ҳ лҳҗлҠ” лі„лҸ„ м„ м–ёмңјлЎң м§Җм •н•ң лӘЁл“Ҳмқҙ лЈЁнҠё лӘЁл“Ҳмқҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

``shell
mkdir test && cd test
`

`hcl title="main.tf"
terraform {
 required_providers {
 aws = {
 source = "hashicorp/aws"
 version = ">= 4.8, = 1.7,"
 key = "/terraform.tfstate"
 region = "us-west-2"
 dynamodb_table = "-locks" # LockID: /-md5
 encrypt = true
 }
}

provider "aws" {
 region = "us-west-2"
 profile = ""
}
`

`shell
terraform init
`

`hcl title="ec2.tf"
resource "aws_instance" "app_server" {
 ami = "ami-090717c950a5c34d3"
 instance_type = "t3.micro"
 tags = {
 "Name" = "app_server"
 }
}

output "instance_id" {
 value = aws_instance.app_server.id
}

output "instance_public_ip" {
 value = aws_instance.app_server.public_ip
}
`

`shell
terraform refresh
`

`shell
terraform apply
`

`shell
terraform destroy
``

title: X.509
text:
``shell
openssl version -a
`

## Root CA

### root-ca.key(Private Key)

`shell
openssl genrsa -out root-ca.key 3072
`

`shell
openssl ecparam -genkey -name prime256v1 -noout -out root-ca.key
`

### root-ca.conf

`ini
[ req ]
default_md = sha256
default_bits = 3072
encrypt_key = no
prompt = no
utf8 = yes

req_extensions = req_ext
x509_extensions = req_ext

distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name

[ req_ext ]
basicConstraints = critical, CA:true
keyUsage = critical, digitalSignature, nonRepudiation, keyEncipherment, keyCertSign

subjectKeyIdentifier = hash
subjectAltName=@san

[ san ]
DNS.1 = loliot.net

[ req_distinguished_name ]
organizationName = lol-iot
organizationalUnitName = devops
countryName = KR
`

- commonName -\> CN
- organizationName -\> O
- organizationalUnitName -\> OU
- localityName -\> L
- stateOrProvinceName -\> ST
- countryName -\> C
- emailAddress -\> EMAIL

- мқёмҰқм„ңмқҳ CN(Common Name)мқҙ UserлЎң мқёмӢқлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.
- мқёмҰқм„ңмқҳ O(Organization)к°Җ GroupмңјлЎң мқёмӢқлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

### root-ca.csr(CSR, мқёмҰқ м„ңлӘ… мҡ”мІӯм„ң)

`shell
openssl req -new -key root-ca.key -config root-ca.conf -out root-ca.csr
`

### root-ca.crt(CRT, мқёмҰқм„ң)

`shell
openssl x509 -req -days 7300 -signkey root-ca.key \
 -extensions req_ext -extfile root-ca.conf \
 -in root-ca.csr -out root-ca.crt
`

`shell
openssl x509 -in root-ca.crt -text -noout
`

## Intermediate CA

### intermediate-ca.key

`shell
openssl genrsa -out intermediate-ca.key 3072
`

`shell
openssl ecparam -genkey -name prime256v1 -noout -out intermediate-ca.key
`

### intermediate-ca.conf

`ini
[ req ]
default_md = sha256
default_bits = 3072
encrypt_key = no
prompt = no
utf8 = yes

req_extensions = req_ext
x509_extensions = req_ext

distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name

[ req_ext ]
basicConstraints = critical, CA:true, pathlen:0
keyUsage = critical, digitalSignature, nonRepudiation, keyEncipherment, keyCertSign

subjectKeyIdentifier = hash
subjectAltName=@san

[ san ]
DNS.1 = loliot.net

[ req_distinguished_name ]
organizationName = lol-iot
organizationalUnitName = devops
countryName = KR
localityName = Seoul
`

### intermediate-ca.csr

`shell
openssl req -new -config intermediate-ca.conf -key intermediate-ca.key -out intermediate-ca.csr
`

### intermediate-ca.crt

`shell
openssl x509 -req -days 1825 \
 -CA root-ca.crt -CAkey root-ca.key -CAcreateserial \
 -extensions req_ext -extfile intermediate-ca.conf \
 -in intermediate-ca.csr -out intermediate-ca.crt
`

`shell
openssl x509 -in intermediate-ca.crt -text -noout
`

### cert-chain.crt

`shell
cat intermediate-ca.crt ../root-ca.crt > cert-chain.crt
``

utf8 = yes

x509_extensions = req_ext

distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name

`yaml title="Kubernetes default JSON parser"
customConfig:
 transforms:
 default_k8s_json_parser:
 type: remap
 inputs: []
 source: |
 .app = .kubernetes.pod_labels."app.kubernetes.io/name"
 .host = "$VECTOR_SELF_NODE_NAME"

 structured, err = parse_json(.message)
 if err == null {
 . = merge!(., structured)
 msg, err = get(., ["msg"])
 if err == null {
 .message = msg
 del(.msg)
 }
 time, err = get(., ["time"])
 if err == null {
 .timestamp = time
 del(.time)
 }
 } else {
 .level = "warn"
 }

 del(.file)
 del(.source_type)
 del(.stream)
 del(.kubernetes)
`

text:
## vLLM мҳөм…ҳ

- vLLM / Configuration / Optimization and Tuning

- ModelConfig
 - --max-model-len
 - promptмҷҖ мғқм„ұлҗң нҶ нҒ°мқ„ нҸ¬н•Ён•ң мөңлҢҖ мӢңнҖҖмҠӨ кёёмқҙмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - м„Өм •н•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡмңјл©ҙ лӘЁлҚём—җ м„Өм •лҗҳм–ҙ мһҲлҠ” мөңлҢҖ кёёмқҙлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
- SchedulerConfig
 - schedule н•ЁмҲҳлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©ҙ н•ң stepм—җ мІҳлҰ¬н•  мҡ”мІӯл“Өмқ„ кІ°м •н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - --max-num-seqs
 - н•ң stepм—җ мІҳлҰ¬н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” мөңлҢҖ мҡ”мІӯ мҲҳ мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - кё°ліёк°’мқҖ 128мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - к°’мқҙ лҶ’м•„м§Җл©ҙ max throughputмқҙ мҰқк°Җн•  мҲҳ мһҲм§Җл§Ң, ITLлҸ„ мҰқк°Җн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - --max-num-batched-tokens
 - н•ң stepм—җ мІҳлҰ¬н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” мөңлҢҖ нҶ нҒ° мҲҳмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - кё°ліёк°’мқҖ 2048мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - к°’мқҙ мһ‘м•„м§Җл©ҙ(e.g. 2048) decodeлҘј лҠҗлҰ¬кІҢ л§Ңл“ңлҠ” prefillмқҙ к°җмҶҢн•ҳм—¬ ITLмқҙ к°җмҶҢн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - к°’мқҙ м»Өм§Җл©ҙ н•ң stepм—җ мІҳлҰ¬н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” prefillмқҙ мҰқк°Җн•ҳм—¬ TTFTк°Җ к°җмҶҢн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - мһ‘мқҖ лӘЁлҚёмқ„ нҒ° GPUм—җм„ң мӢӨн–үн•ҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ° 8192 мқҙмғҒмңјлЎң м„Өм •н•ҳлҠ” кІғмқҙ мўӢмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - --enable-chunked-prefill, --no-enable-chunked-prefill
 - chunked prefillмқ„ нҷңм„ұнҷ”/л№„нҷңм„ұнҷ”н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - кё°ліёк°’мқҖ нҷңм„ұнҷ”мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - chunked prefillмқҙ нҷңм„ұнҷ”лҗҳл©ҙ schedulerлҠ” prefillліҙлӢӨ decodeмқҳ мҡ°м„ мҲңмң„лҘј лҶ’кІҢ м„Өм •н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - batchм—җ decodeлҘј мұ„мҡ°кі  лӮЁмқҖ кіөк°„м—җ prefillмқ„ мұ„мҡё л•Ң кіөк°„мқҙ л¶ҖмЎұн•ҳл©ҙ prefillмқ„ chunkлЎң лӮҳлҲ„м–ҙ мІҳлҰ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

## debhelper

debhelperлҠ” dh, dh_ лӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң лҚ°л№„м•Ҳ нҢЁнӮӨм§ҖлҘј л§Ңл“Өм–ҙмЈјлҠ” нҲҙмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.

dh лӘ…л №м–ҙм—җ мқҳн•ҙ dh_ лӘ…л №м–ҙк°Җ м •н•ҙм§„ мҲңм„ңлЎң мӢӨн–үлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

``shell
dh [options]
`

 - build, build-arch, build-indep

title: Debian Launchpad PPA
text:
## OpenPGP keys

Ref: https://help.ubuntu.com/community/GnuPrivacyGuardHowto

### Generating key

``shell {1}
$ gpg --gen-key

...
Real name: Hyeonki Hong
Email address: hhk7734@gmail.com
...

pub rsa3072 2019-09-08 [SC] [expires: 2021-09-07]
 XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX YYYY YYYY
uid Hyeonki Hong
sub rsa3072 2019-09-08 [E] [expires: 2021-09-07]
`

л§Ҳм§Җл§ү 8мһҗлҰ¬лҘј нӮӨм„ңлІ„лЎң ліҙлғ…лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
gpg --send-keys --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com YYYYYYYY
`

### Importing key

`shell {1}
$ gpg --fingerprint
pub rsa3072 2019-09-08 [SC] [expires: 2021-09-07]
 XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX YYYY YYYY
uid Hyeonki Hong
sub rsa3072 2019-09-08 [E] [expires: 2021-09-07]
`

https://launchpad.net/ -> your page -> OpenPGP keys

мң„мқҳ public keyлҘј ліөмӮ¬н•ң нӣ„, OpenPGP keys нҺҳмқҙм§Җмқҳ **Fingerprint**м—җ л¶ҷм—¬л„Јкі  **Import Key** лІ„нҠјмқ„ лҲ„лҰ…лӢҲлӢӨ.

`text
Fingerprint: XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX YYYY YYYY
`

## PPA

https://launchpad.net/ -> your page

**Create a new PPA** лІ„нҠјмқ„ лҲ„лҘҙкұ°лӮҳ, кё°мЎҙ PPAлҘј м„ нғқн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

### Setting processors

**Change details**м—җ л“Өм–ҙк°Җм„ң PPAм—җ л§һлҠ” н”„лЎңм„ём„ңлҘј м„ нғқ нӣ„ м ҖмһҘн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

### Import

https://code.launchpad.net/ -> Code -> Import your project

### Recipes

Ref: https://help.launchpad.net/Packaging/SourceBuilds/Recipes

https://launchpad.net/ -> your page -> Code [-> View Git repositories]

мҲҳм •н•  лҰ¬нҸ¬м§ҖнҶ лҰ¬лҘј м„ нғқн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

**Create packaging recipe** лІ„нҠјмқ„ лҲ„лҘҙкұ°лӮҳ кё°мЎҙ recipeлҘј м„ нғқн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

#### Edit recipe -> Recipe text

`text
# git-build-recipe format 0.4 deb-version {debversion}~{revtime}
xxx [branch лӘ…]
`

#### Requesting build

**Requesting build** лІ„нҠјмқ„ лҲ„лҘҙл©ҙ Recipe textмҷҖ лҰ¬нҸ¬м§ҖнҶ лҰ¬мқҳ debian л””л үн„°лҰ¬лҘј м°ёкі н•ҳм—¬ л№Ңл“ң мҡ”мІӯмқ„ н•ҳкІҢ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. мһ‘м—… мҲңм„ңм—җ л”°лқј лӘҮмӢңк°„ нӣ„м—җ м§„н–үлҗ  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

л№Ңл“ңк°Җ лҒқлӮҳл©ҙ кІҖнҶ  нӣ„ мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң ppaм—җ л“ұлЎқлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

### PPA registration

м•„лһҳ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј нҶөн•ҙ PPAлҘј л“ұлЎқн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
sudo add-apt-repository ppa:/
`

л“ұлЎқ нӣ„ м—…лҚ°мқҙнҠёлҘј н•ҳл©ҙ, PPAм—җ мһҲлҠ” нҢЁнӮӨм§ҖлҘј лӢӨмҡҙл°ӣмқ„ мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
sudo apt-get update
``

title: Debian Package Makefile
text:
## Basic

``makefile
.PHONY: all
all:

.PHONY: install
install:

.PHONY: clean
clean:

.PHONY: distclean
distclean: clean

.PHONY: uninstall
uninstall:
`

## Dynamic library

Ref: https://github.com/hhk7734/lot-SBC/blob/master/Makefile

`makefile
prefix = /usr

VERSION = $(shell head -n1 debian/changelog | sed 's/.* .\([0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\).*/\1/')
MAJOR = $(shell echo $(VERSION) | sed 's/\([0-9]*\)\..*/\1/')

DYNAMIC_LIB = lib.so

BUILD_DIR = build

CC = $(CROSS_COMPILE)gcc
CXX = $(CROSS_COMPILE)g++
LD = $(CROSS_COMPILE)ld

DEBUG = -g
COMMON_FLAGS = -Wall -fPIC $(DEBUG)
COMMON_FLAGS += -O1
COMMON_FLAGS += -MMD -MP -MF"$(@:%.o=%.d)"

CFLAGS = $(COMMON_FLAGS)

CXXFLAGS = $(COMMON_FLAGS)
CXXFLAGS += -std=c++11

LIBS =

rwildcard=$(wildcard $1$2) $(foreach d,$(wildcard $1*),$(call rwildcard,$d/,$2))

CSRCS =

CXXSRCS =

INCS_DIR = -I.

DEV_HEADERS = $(shell ls /*.h)
DEV_HEADERS += $(shell ls /*.hpp)

.PHONY: all
all: $(DYNAMIC_LIB).$(VERSION)

$(BUILD_DIR):
 mkdir $@

vpath %.c $(sort $(dir $(CSRCS)))
$(BUILD_DIR)/%.o: %.c | $(BUILD_DIR)
 $(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS_DIR) -Wa,-a,-ad,-alms=$(@:.o=.lst) -c -o $@ $.*
``

title: Git commit message
text:
## Message

- https://www.conventionalcommits.org/ko/v1.0.0/#%EA%B0%9C%EC%9A%94

``xml
[()]:

[]

[]
`

-
 - fix: лІ„к·ё мҲҳм • (SemVer, PATCH)
 - feat: мғҲлЎңмҡҙ кё°лҠҘ (SemVer, MINOR)
 - !: !лҠ” BREAKING CHANGEлҘј мқҳлҜён•©лӢҲлӢӨ. (SemVer, MAJOR)
 - perf: м„ұлҠҘ к°ңм„ 
 - test: н…ҢмҠӨнҠё мҪ”л“ң м¶”к°Җ, мҲҳм •
 - refactor: feat, fixк°Җ м•„лӢҢ мҪ”л“ң мҲҳм •
 - docs: л¬ём„ңл§Ң мҲҳм •
 - chore: лӢӨлҘё нғҖмһ…м—җ н•ҙлӢ№н•ҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ” ліҖкІҪмӮ¬н•ӯ
- : ліҖкІҪлҗң мҪ”л“ң лІ”мң„лҘј лӮҳнғҖлӮҙлҠ” лӘЁл“  кІғмңјлЎң мғқлһөмқҙ к°ҖлҠҘн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
-
 - лӘ…л №нҳ•мқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - мҶҢл¬ёмһҗлЎң мӢңмһ‘н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - .(кө¬л‘җм җ)мңјлЎң лҒқлӮҳм§Җ м•ҠмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
-
-
 - git tailer format
 - : нҳ•мӢқмңјлЎң мһ‘м„ұн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - м—җ кіөл°ұмқҙ л“Өм–ҙк°„ кІҪмҡ° кіөл°ұ лҢҖмӢ  -лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - BREAKING CHANGE: лҠ” мҳҲмҷёмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.

н—ӨлҚ”лҠ” 50мһҗ, ліёл¬ёмқҖ 72мһҗ мӨ„ кёёмқҙ(line length)лҘј к¶ҢмһҘн•ҳлҠ” кёҖмқҙ л§ҺмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

title: Git config
text:
## git config

``shell
git config
`

-
 - --system: мӢңмҠӨн…ң м„Өм •(/etc/gitconfig)
 - --global: мң м Җ м„Өм •(~/.gitconfig)
 - --local: н”„лЎңм қнҠё м„Өм •(.git/config)

## url м„Өм •

### insteadOfлЎң url к°’ ліҖнҷҳ

`shell
git config url..insteadOf
`

clone, fetch, push л“ұмқ„ мҲҳн–үн•  л•Ң, мһ…л Ҙн•ң URLмқҙлӮҳ м„Өм •н•ң URL мӨ‘ лЎң мӢңмһ‘н•ҳлҠ” URLмқҙ мһҲлӢӨл©ҙ лЎң ліҖнҷҳн•ң нӣ„ лӘ…л №мқ„ мҲҳн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

мҳҲлҘј л“Өм–ҙ нҠ№м • н”„лЎңк·ёлһЁмқҙ gitмңјлЎң https:/github.com кІҪлЎңмқҳ лҰ¬нҸ¬м§ҖнҶ лҰ¬м—җ м ‘к·јн•  л•Ң, н•ҙлӢ№ н”„лЎңк·ёлһЁ мҲҳм • м—Ҷмқҙ https лҢҖмӢ  sshлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳлҸ„лЎқ м„Өм •н•ҳкі  мӢ¶лӢӨл©ҙ м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©ҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
git config --global url.ssh://git@github.com/.insteadOf https://github.com/
`

## Signing м„Өм •

### SSH signing

`shell
git config --global gpg.format ssh
`

`shell
git config --global user.signingkey
`

`shell
git config --global commit.gpgsign true
git config --global tag.gpgsign true
`

`shell title="~/.ssh/allowed_signers"
[,] [namespace="[,]"]
# hhk7734@gmail.com ssh-ed25519 AAAA...
`

`shell
git config --global gpg.ssh.allowedSignersFile ~/.ssh/allowed_signers
`

## кё°нғҖ

- git config --global branch.sort -committerdate
- git config --global diff.algorithm histogram
- git config --global pull.rebase true
- git config --global push.autoSetupRemote true
- git config --global push.default simple: лёҢлһңм№ҳлҘј нҳ„мһ¬ лёҢлһңм№ҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ мқҙлҰ„мқҳ мӣҗкІ© лёҢлһңм№ҳлҘј м„Өм •н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
- git config --global rebase.autoStash true
- git config --global status.submoduleSummary true: submoduleмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•  л•Ң, statusм—җм„ң submodule ліҖкІҪмӮ¬н•ӯмқ„ мҡ”м•Ҫн•ҙм„ң ліҙм—¬мӨҚлӢҲлӢӨ.
- git config --global tag.sort version:refname`: tagлҘј м •л ¬н•  л•Ң, лІ„м „ мҲңмңјлЎң м •л ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

title: pre-commitмңјлЎң git hook кҙҖлҰ¬н•ҳкё°
text:
## м„Өм№ҳ

``shell
python3 -m pip install pre-commit
`

## commands

### pre-commit install

`shell
pre-commit install
`

мң„ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©ҙ н•ҙлӢ№ лҰ¬нҸ¬м§ҖнҶ лҰ¬мқҳ .git/hooksм—җ pre-commitмқ„ м„Өм№ҳн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

- кё°ліём ҒмңјлЎң ~/.cache/pre-commit мң„м№ҳм—җ мӮ¬мҡ©н•  лӘЁл“Ҳмқҙ м ҖмһҘлҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ
- PRE_COMMIT_HOMEлҘј м„ м–ён•ҳм—¬ м„Өм№ҳ мң„м№ҳлҘј ліҖкІҪн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ

### pre-commit init-templatedir

- https://pre-commit.com/#pre-commit-init-templatedir

`shell
git config --global init.templateDir ~/.git-template
pre-commit init-templatedir ~/.git-template -t pre-commit -t prepare-commit-msg
`

git config init.templateDirкіј н•Ёк»ҳ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳм—¬ git initмқҙлӮҳ git clone мӢң мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң pre-commitмқ„ м„Өм№ҳн•ҳлҸ„лЎқ н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

### pre-commit run

`shell
pre-commit run []
`

- flags

title: Build external module
text:
- https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/kbuild/modules.txt

``c
#include
#include
#include

static int __init hello_init(void) {
 printk(KERN_INFO "hello_init\n");
 return 0;
}

static void __exit hello_exit(void) { printk(KERN_INFO "hello_exit\n"); }

module_init(hello_init);
module_exit(hello_exit);

MODULE_LICENSE("GPL");
MODULE_AUTHOR("Hyeonki Hong ");
MODULE_DESCRIPTION("Print init and exit");
`

`makefile
ifneq ($(KERNELRELEASE),)

obj-m := hello.o

else

KERNELDIR ?= /lib/modules/$(shell uname -r)/build
PWD := $(shell pwd)

modules:
 $(MAKE) -C $(KERNELDIR) M=$(PWD) modules

modules_install:
 $(MAKE) -C $(KERNELDIR) M=$(PWD) modules_install

clean:
 $(MAKE) -C $(KERNELDIR) M=$(PWD) clean

endif
`

`

sudo depmod

`

`text
Building External Modules

This document describes how to build an out-of-tree kernel module.

=== Table of Contents

 === 1 Introduction
 === 2 How to Build External Modules
 --- 2.1 Command Syntax
 --- 2.2 Options
 --- 2.3 Targets
 --- 2.4 Building Separate Files
 === 3. Creating a Kbuild File for an External Module
 --- 3.1 Shared Makefile
 --- 3.2 Separate Kbuild file and Makefile
 --- 3.3 Binary Blobs
 --- 3.4 Building Multiple Modules
 === 4. Include Files
 --- 4.1 Kernel Includes
 --- 4.2 Single Subdirectory
 --- 4.3 Several Subdirectories
 === 5. Module Installation
 --- 5.1 INSTALL_MOD_PATH
 --- 5.2 INSTALL_MOD_DIR
 === 6. Module Versioning
 --- 6.1 Symbols From the Kernel (vmlinux + modules)
 --- 6.2 Symbols and External Modules
 --- 6.3 Symbols From Another External Module
 === 7. Tips & Tricks
 --- 7.1 Testing for CONFIG_FOO_BAR

=== 1. Introduction

"kbuild" is the build system used by the Linux kernel. Modules must use
kbuild to stay compatible with changes in the build infrastructure and
to pick up the right flags to "gcc." Functionality for building modules
both in-tree and out-of-tree is provided. The method for building
either is similar, and all modules are initially developed and built
out-of-tree.

Covered in this document is information aimed at developers interested
in building out-of-tree (or "external") modules. The author of an
external module should supply a makefile that hides most of the
complexity, so one only has to type "make" to build the module. This is
easily accomplished, and a complete example will be presented in
section 3.

=== 2. How to Build External Modules

To build external modules, you must have a prebuilt kernel available
that contains the configuration and header files used in the build.
Also, the kernel must have been built with modules enabled. If you are
using a distribution kernel, there will be a package for the kernel you
are running provided by your distribution.

An alternative is to use the "make" target "modules_prepare." This will
make sure the kernel contains the information required. The target
exists solely as a simple way to prepare a kernel source tree for
building external modules.

NOTE: "modules_prepare" will not build Module.symvers even if
CONFIG_MODVERSIONS is set; therefore, a full kernel build needs to be
executed to make module versioning work.

--- 2.1 Command Syntax

 The command to build an external module is:

 $ make -C M=$PWD

 The kbuild system knows that an external module is being built
 due to the "M=" option given in the command.

 To build against the running kernel use:

 $ make -C /lib/modules/uname -r/build M=$PWD

 Then to install the module(s) just built, add the target
 "modules_install" to the command:

 $ make -C /lib/modules/uname -r/build M=$PWD modules_install

--- 2.2 Options

 ($KDIR refers to the path of the kernel source directory.)

 make -C $KDIR M=$PWD

 -C $KDIR
 The directory where the kernel source is located.
 "make" will actually change to the specified directory
 when executing and will change back when finished.

 M=$PWD
 Informs kbuild that an external module is being built.
 The value given to "M" is the absolute path of the
 directory where the external module (kbuild file) is
 located.

--- 2.3 Targets

 When building an external module, only a subset of the "make"
 targets are available.

 make -C $KDIR M=$PWD [target]

 The default will build the module(s) located in the current
 directory, so a target does not need to be specified. All
 output files will also be generated in this directory. No
 attempts are made to update the kernel source, and it is a
 precondition that a successful "make" has been executed for the
 kernel.

 modules
 The default target for external modules. It has the
 same functionality as if no target was specified. See
 description above.

 modules_install
 Install the external module(s). The default location is
 /lib/modules//extra/, but a prefix may
 be added with INSTALL_MOD_PATH (discussed in section 5).

 clean
 Remove all generated files in the module directory only.

 help
 List the available targets for external modules.

--- 2.4 Building Separate Files

 It is possible to build single files that are part of a module.
 This works equally well for the kernel, a module, and even for
 external modules.

 Example (The module foo.ko, consist of bar.o and baz.o):
 make -C $KDIR M=$PWD bar.lst
 make -C $KDIR M=$PWD baz.o
 make -C $KDIR M=$PWD foo.ko
 make -C $KDIR M=$PWD ./

=== 3. Creating a Kbuild File for an External Module

In the last section we saw the command to build a module for the
running kernel. The module is not actually built, however, because a
build file is required. Contained in this file will be the name of
the module(s) being built, along with the list of requisite source
files. The file may be as simple as a single line:

 obj-m := .o

The kbuild system will build .o from .c,
and, after linking, will result in the kernel module .ko.
The above line can be put in either a "Kbuild" file or a "Makefile."
When the module is built from multiple sources, an additional line is
needed listing the files:

 -y := .o .o ...

NOTE: Further documentation describing the syntax used by kbuild is
located in Documentation/kbuild/makefiles.txt.

The examples below demonstrate how to create a build file for the
module 8123.ko, which is built from the following files:

 8123_if.c
 8123_if.h
 8123_pci.c
 8123_bin.o_shipped filename: Makefile
 ifneq ($(KERNELRELEASE),)
 # kbuild part of makefile
 obj-m := 8123.o
 8123-y := 8123_if.o 8123_pci.o 8123_bin.o

 else
 # normal makefile
 KDIR ?= /lib/modules/uname -r/build

 default:
 $(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$$PWD

 # Module specific targets
 genbin:
 echo "X" > 8123_bin.o_shipped

 endif

 The check for KERNELRELEASE is used to separate the two parts
 of the makefile. In the example, kbuild will only see the two
 assignments, whereas "make" will see everything except these
 two assignments. This is due to two passes made on the file:
 the first pass is by the "make" instance run on the command
 line; the second pass is by the kbuild system, which is
 initiated by the parameterized "make" in the default target.

--- 3.2 Separate Kbuild File and Makefile

 In newer versions of the kernel, kbuild will first look for a
 file named "Kbuild," and only if that is not found, will it
 then look for a makefile. Utilizing a "Kbuild" file allows us
 to split up the makefile from example 1 into two files:

 Example 2:
 --> filename: Kbuild
 obj-m := 8123.o
 8123-y := 8123_if.o 8123_pci.o 8123_bin.o

 --> filename: Makefile
 KDIR ?= /lib/modules/uname -r/build

 default:
 $(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$$PWD

 # Module specific targets
 genbin:
 echo "X" > 8123_bin.o_shipped

 The split in example 2 is questionable due to the simplicity of
 each file; however, some external modules use makefiles
 consisting of several hundred lines, and here it really pays
 off to separate the kbuild part from the rest.

 The next example shows a backward compatible version.

 Example 3:
 --> filename: Kbuild
 obj-m := 8123.o
 8123-y := 8123_if.o 8123_pci.o 8123_bin.o

 --> filename: Makefile
 ifneq ($(KERNELRELEASE),)
 # kbuild part of makefile
 include Kbuild

 else
 # normal makefile
 KDIR ?= /lib/modules/uname -r/build

 default:
 $(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$$PWD

 # Module specific targets
 genbin:
 echo "X" > 8123_bin.o_shipped

 endif

 Here the "Kbuild" file is included from the makefile. This
 allows an older version of kbuild, which only knows of
 makefiles, to be used when the "make" and kbuild parts are
 split into separate files.

--- 3.3 Binary Blobs

 Some external modules need to include an object file as a blob.
 kbuild has support for this, but requires the blob file to be
 named _shipped. When the kbuild rules kick in, a copy
 of _shipped is created with _shipped stripped off,
 giving us . This shortened filename can be used in
 the assignment to the module.

 Throughout this section, 8123_bin.o_shipped has been used to
 build the kernel module 8123.ko; it has been included as
 8123_bin.o.

 8123-y := 8123_if.o 8123_pci.o 8123_bin.o

 Although there is no distinction between the ordinary source
 files and the binary file, kbuild will pick up different rules
 when creating the object file for the module.

--- 3.4 Building Multiple Modules

 kbuild supports building multiple modules with a single build
 file. For example, if you wanted to build two modules, foo.ko
 and bar.ko, the kbuild lines would be:

 obj-m := foo.o bar.o
 foo-y :=
 bar-y :=

 It is that simple!

=== 4. Include Files

Within the kernel, header files are kept in standard locations
according to the following rule:

 * If the header file only describes the internal interface of a
 module, then the file is placed in the same directory as the
 source files.
 * If the header file describes an interface used by other parts
 of the kernel that are located in different directories, then
 the file is placed in include/linux/.

 NOTE: There are two notable exceptions to this rule: larger
 subsystems have their own directory under include/, such as
 include/scsi; and architecture specific headers are located
 under arch/$(ARCH)/include/.

--- 4.1 Kernel Includes

 To include a header file located under include/linux/, simply
 use:

 #include

 kbuild will add options to "gcc" so the relevant directories
 are searched.

--- 4.2 Single Subdirectory

 External modules tend to place header files in a separate
 include/ directory where their source is located, although this
 is not the usual kernel style. To inform kbuild of the
 directory, use either ccflags-y or CFLAGS_.o.

 Using the example from section 3, if we moved 8123_if.h to a
 subdirectory named include, the resulting kbuild file would
 look like:

 --> filename: Kbuild
 obj-m := 8123.o

 ccflags-y := -Iinclude
 8123-y := 8123_if.o 8123_pci.o 8123_bin.o

 Note that in the assignment there is no space between -I and
 the path. This is a limitation of kbuild: there must be no
 space present.

--- 4.3 Several Subdirectories

 kbuild can handle files that are spread over several directories.
 Consider the following example:

 .
 |__ src
 | |__ complex_main.c
 | |__ hal
 | |__ hardwareif.c
 | |__ include
 | |__ hardwareif.h
 |__ include
 |__ complex.h

 To build the module complex.ko, we then need the following
 kbuild file:

 --> filename: Kbuild
 obj-m := complex.o
 complex-y := src/complex_main.o
 complex-y += src/hal/hardwareif.o

 ccflags-y := -I$(src)/include
 ccflags-y += -I$(src)/src/hal/include

 As you can see, kbuild knows how to handle object files located
 in other directories. The trick is to specify the directory
 relative to the kbuild file's location. That being said, this
 is NOT recommended practice.

 For the header files, kbuild must be explicitly told where to
 look. When kbuild executes, the current directory is always the
 root of the kernel tree (the argument to "-C") and therefore an
 absolute path is needed. $(src) provides the absolute path by
 pointing to the directory where the currently executing kbuild
 file is located.

=== 5. Module Installation

Modules which are included in the kernel are installed in the
directory:

 /lib/modules/$(KERNELRELEASE)/kernel/

And external modules are installed in:

 /lib/modules/$(KERNELRELEASE)/extra/

--- 5.1 INSTALL_MOD_PATH

 Above are the default directories but as always some level of
 customization is possible. A prefix can be added to the
 installation path using the variable INSTALL_MOD_PATH:

 $ make INSTALL_MOD_PATH=/frodo modules_install
 => Install dir: /frodo/lib/modules/$(KERNELRELEASE)/kernel/

 INSTALL_MOD_PATH may be set as an ordinary shell variable or,
 as shown above, can be specified on the command line when
 calling "make." This has effect when installing both in-tree
 and out-of-tree modules.

--- 5.2 INSTALL_MOD_DIR

 External modules are by default installed to a directory under
 /lib/modules/$(KERNELRELEASE)/extra/, but you may wish to
 locate modules for a specific functionality in a separate
 directory. For this purpose, use INSTALL_MOD_DIR to specify an
 alternative name to "extra."

 $ make INSTALL_MOD_DIR=gandalf -C $KDIR \
 M=$PWD modules_install
 => Install dir: /lib/modules/$(KERNELRELEASE)/gandalf/

=== 6. Module Versioning

Module versioning is enabled by the CONFIG_MODVERSIONS tag, and is used
as a simple ABI consistency check. A CRC value of the full prototype
for an exported symbol is created. When a module is loaded/used, the
CRC values contained in the kernel are compared with similar values in
the module; if they are not equal, the kernel refuses to load the
module.

Module.symvers contains a list of all exported symbols from a kernel
build.

--- 6.1 Symbols From the Kernel (vmlinux + modules)

 During a kernel build, a file named Module.symvers will be
 generated. Module.symvers contains all exported symbols from
 the kernel and compiled modules. For each symbol, the
 corresponding CRC value is also stored.

 The syntax of the Module.symvers file is:

 0x2d036834 scsi_remove_host drivers/scsi/scsi_mod

 For a kernel build without CONFIG_MODVERSIONS enabled, the CRC
 would read 0x00000000.

 Module.symvers serves two purposes:
 1) It lists all exported symbols from vmlinux and all modules.
 2) It lists the CRC if CONFIG_MODVERSIONS is enabled.

--- 6.2 Symbols and External Modules

 When building an external module, the build system needs access
 to the symbols from the kernel to check if all external symbols
 are defined. This is done in the MODPOST step. modpost obtains
 the symbols by reading Module.symvers from the kernel source
 tree. If a Module.symvers file is present in the directory
 where the external module is being built, this file will be
 read too. During the MODPOST step, a new Module.symvers file
 will be written containing all exported symbols that were not
 defined in the kernel.

--- 6.3 Symbols From Another External Module

 Sometimes, an external module uses exported symbols from
 another external module. kbuild needs to have full knowledge of
 all symbols to avoid spitting out warnings about undefined
 symbols. Three solutions exist for this situation.

 NOTE: The method with a top-level kbuild file is recommended
 but may be impractical in certain situations.

 Use a top-level kbuild file
 If you have two modules, foo.ko and bar.ko, where
 foo.ko needs symbols from bar.ko, you can use a
 common top-level kbuild file so both modules are
 compiled in the same build. Consider the following
 directory layout:

 ./foo/ <= contains foo.ko
 ./bar/ <= contains bar.ko

 The top-level kbuild file would then look like:

 #./Kbuild (or ./Makefile):
 obj-y := foo/ bar/

 And executing

 $ make -C $KDIR M=$PWD

 will then do the expected and compile both modules with
 full knowledge of symbols from either module.

 Use an extra Module.symvers file
 When an external module is built, a Module.symvers file
 is generated containing all exported symbols which are
 not defined in the kernel. To get access to symbols
 from bar.ko, copy the Module.symvers file from the
 compilation of bar.ko to the directory where foo.ko is
 built. During the module build, kbuild will read the
 Module.symvers file in the directory of the external
 module, and when the build is finished, a new
 Module.symvers file is created containing the sum of
 all symbols defined and not part of the kernel.

 Use "make" variable KBUILD_EXTRA_SYMBOLS
 If it is impractical to copy Module.symvers from
 another module, you can assign a space separated list
 of files to KBUILD_EXTRA_SYMBOLS in your build file.
 These files will be loaded by modpost during the
 initialization of its symbol tables.

=== 7. Tips & Tricks

--- 7.1 Testing for CONFIG_FOO_BAR

 Modules often need to check for certain CONFIG_ options to
 decide if a specific feature is included in the module. In
 kbuild this is done by referencing the CONFIG_ variable
 directly.

 #fs/ext2/Makefile
 obj-$(CONFIG_EXT2_FS) += ext2.o

 ext2-y := balloc.o bitmap.o dir.o
 ext2-$(CONFIG_EXT2_FS_XATTR) += xattr.o

 External modules have traditionally used "grep" to check for
 specific CONFIG_ settings directly in .config. This usage is
 broken. As introduced before, external modules should use
 kbuild for building and can therefore use the same methods as
 in-tree modules when testing for CONFIG_ definitions.
``

title: ftrace
text:
## Linux kernel configuration

Ref: https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/trace/ftrace.txt

### \\_defconfig

- kernel/trace/Kconfig: м„Өм • к°ҖлҠҘн•ң мҳөм…ҳ, мқҳмЎҙм„ұ, м„ӨлӘ… л“ұмқҙ мһҲмңјлҜҖлЎң м°ёкі н•ҳмӢңкё° л°”лһҚлӢҲлӢӨ.
- arch//configs/_defconfig

#### Options

- **CONFIG_FTRACE=y**: Enables the kernel tracing infrastructure.
 - **CONFIG_FUNCTION_TRACER=y**: Enables the kernel to trace every kernel function.
 - **CONFIG_FUNCTION_GRAPH_TRACER=y**: Enables the kernel to trace a function at both its return and its entry.
 - CONFIG_FUNCTION_PROFILER=y: Enables the kernel function profiler. A file is created in debugfs called function_profile_enabled which defaults to zero. When a 1 is echoed into this file profiling begins, and when a zero is entered, profiling stops. A "functions" file is created in the trace_stats directory; this file shows the list of functions that have been hit and their counters.
 - CONFIG_IRQSOFF_TRACER=y: Measures the time spent in irqs-off critical sections, with microsecond accuracy.
 - CONFIG_SCHED_TRACER=y: Tracks the latency of the highest priority task to be scheduled in, starting from the point it has woken up.
 - CONFIG_TRACER_SNAPSHOT=y: Allows tracing users to take snapshot of the current buffer using the ftrace interface.
 - **CONFIG_STACK_TRACER=y**: Records the maximum stack footprint of the kernel and displays it in /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/stack_trace.
 - **CONFIG_DYNAMIC_FTRACE=y**: This option will modify all the calls to function tracing dynamically (will patch them out of the binary image and replace them with a No-Op instruction) on boot up. During compile time, a table is made of all the locations that ftrace can function trace, and this table is linked into the kernel image. When this is enabled, functions can be individually enabled, and the functions not enabled will not affect performance of the system.

### trace_printk

м—җ м •мқҳлҗҳм–ҙ мһҲлҠ” н•ЁмҲҳмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
Ftrace introduces a new form of printk() called trace_printk(). It can be used just like printk(), and can also be used in any context (interrupt code, NMI code, and scheduler code). What is nice about trace_printk() is that it does not output to the console. Instead it writes to the Ftrace ring buffer and can be read via the trace file.

## ftrace setup

м„Өм •мқ„ мӢңмһ‘н•ҳкё° м „м—җ ftraceлҘј л№„нҷңм„ұнҷ”н•ң нӣ„ м„Өм •н•ҳмӢңкё° л°”лһҚлӢҲлӢӨ.

### ftrace on(restart)/off

``shell
echo 1 > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/tracing_on && sleep 1
`

`shell
echo 0 > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/tracing_on && sleep 1
`

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/tracing_on
`

### ftrace event on/off

`shell
echo 1 > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/events/enable && sleep 1
`

`shell
echo 0 > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/events/enable && sleep 1
`

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/events/enable
`

### set_ftrace_filter

/sys/kernel/debug/tracing/available_filter_functionsм—җ л“ұлЎқлҗң н•ЁмҲҳл§Ң м¶”мІҷ к°ҖлҠҘн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/available_filter_functions
`

formatм—җ л”°лқј н•ЁмҲҳ, лӘЁл“Ҳ л“ұмқ„ м„ нғқм ҒмңјлЎң л“ұлЎқн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

set_ftrace_filterм—җ н•ЁмҲҳлҘј л“ұлЎқн•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡкі  ftraceк°Җ мӢӨн–үлҗҳл©ҙ лӘЁл“  н•ЁмҲҳлҘј м¶”м Ғн•ҳкё° л•Ңл¬ём—җ мӢңмҠӨн…ңм—җ **кіјл¶Җн•ҳ**к°Җ кұёл Ө мҳӨлҸҷмһ‘н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
echo ... > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/set_ftrace_filter
`

format: ::

- function
- command
 - mod: н•ЁмҲҳлҘј лӘЁл“ҲлЎң н•„н„°л§Ғн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - e.g., :mod:spi_gpioлҘј м„Өм •н•ҳл©ҙ spi_gpio кҙҖл Ё лӘЁл“Ҳ лӮҙм—җм„ң мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳлҠ” лӘЁл“  н•ЁмҲҳлҘј м¶”м Ғн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - moduleмқҳ кІҪмҡ° modprobeлӮҳ insmodм—җ мқҳн•ҙ м¶”к°Җлҗҳкё° м „м—җ л“ұлЎқн•  мҲҳ м—ҶмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
 - traceon/traceoff
 - snapshot
 - enable_event/disable_event
 - dump
 - cpudump
- parameter

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/set_ftrace_filter
`

### ftrace tracer setup

/sys/kernel/debug/tracing/available_tracersм—җ л“ұлЎқлҗң tracerл§Ң м„Өм • к°ҖлҠҘн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/available_tracers
`

`shell
echo > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/current_tracer && sleep 1
`

- **nop**: ftrace eventл§Ң м¶ңл Ҙ
- **function**: set_ftrace_filterм—җ л“ұлЎқлҗң н•ЁмҲҳлҘј м–ҙл””м—җм„ң нҳём¶ңн–ҲлҠ”м§Җ м¶ңл Ҙ
- **function_graph**: н•ЁмҲҳ мӢӨн–ү мӢңк°„, м„ёл¶Җ нҳём¶ң м •ліҙ м¶ңл Ҙ

`shell
echo nop > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/current_tracer && sleep 1
`

`shell
echo function > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/current_tracer && sleep 1
`

`shell
echo function_graph > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/current_tracer && sleep 1
`

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/current_tracer
`

## Trace

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/trace
`

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/trace | head -n
`

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/trace | grep -n -C
`

tracerк°Җ ліҖкІҪлҗҳл©ҙ мҙҲкё°нҷ” лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

## Example

spidevмқҳ bus лІҲнҳёк°Җ м–ҙл–»кІҢ м„Өм •лҗҳлҠ”м§Җ нҷ•мқён•ҳлҠ” н…ҢмҠӨнҠё мҳҲм ңмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. н…ҢмҠӨнҠёлҠ” Hardkernel Odroid-C2м—җм„ң м§„н–үн–ҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

### Linux kernel configuration

#### \\_defconfig

`Makefile title="arch//configs/_defconfig"
CONFIG_FTRACE=y
CONFIG_FUNCTION_TRACER=y
CONFIG_FUNCTION_GRAPH_TRACER=y
CONFIG_STACK_TRACER=y
CONFIG_DYNAMIC_FTRACE=y
`

#### Linux kernel driver

`c title="driver/spi/spi.c"
int spi_register_master(struct spi_master *master) {
 ...

 trace_printk("bus_num: %d\n", master->bus_num);
 trace_printk("dev.of_node: %d\n", master->dev.of_node);

 if ((master->bus_num dev.of_node)
 master->bus_num = of_alias_get_id(master->dev.of_node, "spi");

 trace_printk("bus_num: %d\n", master->bus_num);

 /* convention: dynamically assigned bus IDs count down from the max */
 if (master->bus_num bus_num = atomic_dec_return(&dyn_bus_id);
 dynamic = 1;
 }

 trace_printk("bus_num: %d\n", master->bus_num);

 ...
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(spi_register_master);
`

#### Build Linux kernel

### ftrace setup

`shell
echo 0 > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/tracing_on && sleep 1 &&\
echo 0 > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/events/enable && sleep 1 &&\
echo '*spi_*' > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/set_ftrace_filter && sleep 1 &&\
echo nop > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/current_tracer && sleep 1 &&\
echo function_graph > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/current_tracer && sleep 1 &&\
echo 1 > /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/tracing_on && sleep 1
`

### Trace

`shell
cat /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/trace
# tracer: function_graph
#
# CPU DURATION FUNCTION CALLS
# | | | | | | |
 0) + 47.000 us | spi_register_driver();
 ------------------------------------------
 0) modprob-1885 => modprob-1891
 ------------------------------------------

 0) 4.000 us | spi_alloc_master();
 0) | spi_register_master() {
 0) | /* bus_num: -1 */
 0) | /* dev.of_node: 1948176240 */
 0) | /* bus_num: -19 */
 0) | /* bus_num: 32764 */
 0) 1.000 us | spi_start_queue();
 0) 4.000 us | spi_alloc_device();
 ------------------------------------------
 0) modprob-1891 => spi3276-1893
 ------------------------------------------

 0) 1.000 us | spi_pump_messages();
 ------------------------------------------
 0) spi3276-1893 => modprob-1891
 ------------------------------------------

 0) | spi_add_device() {
 0) + 35.000 us | spi_setup();
 0) 1.000 us | spi_uevent();
 0) 5.000 us | spi_match_device();
 0) ! 131.000 us | spi_drv_probe();
 0) ! 318.000 us | }
 0) ! 3811.000 us | } /* spi_register_master */
 1) 2.000 us | spi_uevent();
 1) 2.000 us | spi_uevent();
`

### Consequence

мҲҳм •лҗң spi.c мҪ”л“ңмҷҖ trace лӮҙмҡ©мқ„ л№„көҗн•ҙліҙл©ҙ bus лІҲнҳёк°Җ мІҳмқҢл¶Җн„° н• лӢ№лҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•„ мқҢмҲҳмҳҖкі , of_alias_get_id()н•ЁмҲҳ нҳём¶ң нӣ„м—җлҸ„ н• лӢ№лҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•„ мқҢмҲҳмқё кІғмқ„ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

/* convention: dynamically assigned bus IDs count down from the max */ лқјлҠ” мЈјм„қмқҙ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. л”°лқјм„ң лҸҷм Ғ н• лӢ№лҗң busлҠ” 32766, 32765 л“ұмқҳ лІҲнҳёлҘј н• лӢ№ л°ӣмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

м •м ҒмңјлЎң н• лӢ№н•ҙмЈјкё° мң„н•ҙм„ң of_alias_get_id()м—җ мқҳн•ҙ лІҲнҳёлҘј н• лӢ№ л°ӣмқ„ мҲҳ мһҲкІҢ н•ҳл©ҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

мҳҲлҘј л“Өм–ҙ bus лІҲнҳёлҘј 0мңјлЎң м„Өм •н•ҳкі  мӢ¶мқҖ кІҪмҡ° dtsмқҳ spi л…ёл“ңм—җ spi_0: к°ҷмқҖ лқјлІЁмқ„ л¶ҷм—¬мЈјкі , aliasesм—җ spi0 = &spi_0`лҘј м¶”к°Җн•ҳл©ҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.

Ref: https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/trace/ftrace.txt

### \\_defconfig

- kernel/trace/Kconfig: м„Өм • к°ҖлҠҘн•ң мҳөм…ҳ, мқҳмЎҙм„ұ, м„ӨлӘ… л“ұмқҙ мһҲмңјлҜҖлЎң м°ёкі н•ҳмӢңкё°вҖҰ

title: How to contribute to Linux kernel
description: How to contribute to Linux kernel
text:
## Write commit message

``shell
title

details

Signed-off-by: Hyeonki Hong
`

## Make patch

`shell
git format-patch [-] [commit]
`

`shell
./scripts/checkpatch.pl
`

## Set mutt

`shell title=".muttrc"
unset use_domain
set realname = "Hyeonki Hong"
set from = "hhk7734@gmail.com"
set use_from = yes

set imap_user = "hhk7734@gmail.com"
set imap_pass = ""
set spoolfile = imaps://imap.gmail.com/INBOX
set folder = imaps://imap.gmail.com/
set record="imaps://imap.gmail.com/[Gmail]/Sent Mail"
set postponed="imaps://imap.gmail.com/[Gmail]/Drafts"
set mbox="imaps://imap.gmail.com/[Gmail]/All Mail"

set smtp_url = "smtp://hhk7734@smtp.gmail.com:587/"
set smtp_pass = $imap_pass
set ssl_force_tls = yes

set editor = "vim"
set edit_headers = yes
set charset = UTF-8
`

## Send patch mail

### Get maintainer

`shell
./scripts/get_maintainer.pl
`

### Send patch using mutt

`shell
mutt -H
`

`txt
To: , , ...
`

`txt
Cc: , ...
`

## Reply to comments

`shell
mutt
`

Reply, including the message you received.

Write the answer inline or at the end of the included message.

### Example

`text
From: Hyeonki Hong
Date: Thu, Jun 11, 2020 at 2:40 PM
Subject: [PATCH] pinctrl: meson: fix drive strength register and bit
calculation
To: Jerome Brunet , Linus Walleij ,
Cc: , , ,

On Wed, Jun 10, 2020 at 03:09:42PM +0200, Jerome Brunet wrote:
On Wed 10 Jun 2020 at 06:13, hhk7734@gmail.com wrote:
> From: Hyeonki Hong
>
> If a GPIO bank has greater than 16 pins, PAD_DS_REG is split into two
> registers. However, when register and bit were calculated, the first
> register defined in the bank was used, and the bit was calculated based
> on the first pin. This causes problems in setting the driving strength.
>
> Solved the problem by changing the bit using a mask and selecting the
> next register when the bit exceeds 15.
This fixes the case of GPIOX on g12 which goes up to 18 yes but the same
problem will happen again a if bank ever goes past 31 pins. In such case
the problem would apply to all reg types.
I would prefer if it was solved in a more generic fashion, like defining
a "stride" table with the values of each reg type. This table can common
to all aml SoCs for now but eventually it probably need to be SoC
specific.
This would allow to :
A) handle the case you are reporting in a generic (future proof) way
B) remove the weird "bit = bit regs[reg_type];

 *bit = (desc->bit + pin - bank->first) reg + (*bit / 32)) * 4;
 *bit &= 0x1f;

}

How about this?
> ## Signed-off-by: Hyeonki Hong
>
> drivers/pinctrl/meson/pinctrl-meson.c | 7 +++++++
> 1 file changed, 7 insertions(+)
>
> diff --git a/drivers/pinctrl/meson/pinctrl-meson.c b/drivers/pinctrl/meson/pinctrl-meson.c
> index bbc919bef2bf..ef66239b7df5 100644
> --- a/drivers/pinctrl/meson/pinctrl-meson.c
> +++ b/drivers/pinctrl/meson/pinctrl-meson.c
> @@ -98,6 +98,13 @@ static void meson_calc_reg_and_bit(struct meson_bank \*bank, unsigned int pin,
>
> *reg = desc->reg * 4;
> *bit = desc->bit + pin - bank->first;
>
> -
> - if (reg_type == REG_DS) {
> - if (*bit > 15) {
> - *bit &= 0xf;
> - *reg += 4;
> - }
> - }
> }
>
> static int meson_get_groups_count(struct pinctrl_dev \*pcdev)
``

> register defined in theвҖҰ

title: Null-modem emulator tty0uart
text:
Ref:

- http://jake.dothome.co.kr/tty/
- https://bootlin.com/doc/legacy/serial-drivers/
- https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/linux-device-drivers/0596005903/ch18.html
- https://github.com/martinezjavier/ldd3/blob/master/tty/tiny_tty.c

к°ҖмғҒмңјлЎң н•ҳл“ңмӣЁм–ҙ UART мһҘм№ҳлҘј л§Ңл“Өл Өл©ҙ м•„лһҳ мЎ°кұҙмқҙ л§ҢмЎұлҗҳм–ҙм•ј н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

- /sys/class/tty/xxxмқҳ л§ҒнҒ¬к°Җ /sys/devices/virtual/tty/xxxл©ҙ м•Ҳ лҗЁ
- /sys/class/tty/xxx/device л§ҒнҒ¬к°Җ мЎҙмһ¬н•ҙм•ј н•Ё
- linux/serial_core.h (uart_driver \ tty_driver)
- normal serial ports == N_TTY
- platform_deviceмҷҖ platform_driverмқҳ nameмқҙ к°ҷм•„м•ј м—°кІ° лҗЁ

## Git

Git: https://github.com/hhk7734/tty0uart

## Flow control

- DCD: Data Carrier Detect
- RXD: Receive Data
- TXD: Transmit Data
- DTR: Data Terminal Ready
- DSR: Data Set Ready
- RTS: Request To Send
- CTS: Clear To Send
- CD: Carrier Detect
- RI: Ring Indicator

TXD -> RXD

RTS -> CTS

DTR -> DSR
snippet:
вҖҰeviceмҷҖ platform_driverмқҳ nameмқҙ к°ҷм•„м•ј м—°кІ° лҗЁ

## Git

Git: https://github.com/hhk7734/tty0uart

## Flow control

- DCD: Data Carrier Detect
- RXD: Receive Data

title: Linux User Namespace
text:
## User Namespace

- https://www.man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/user_namespaces.7.html
User namespaces isolate security-related identifiers and attributes, in particular, user IDs and group IDs (see credentials(7)), the root directory, keys (see keyrings(7)), and capabilities (see capabilities(7)).

мң м Җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨлҠ” ліҙм•Ҳ кҙҖл Ё мӢқлі„мһҗ л°Ҹ мҶҚм„ұ, нҠ№нһҲ UIDмҷҖ GID, лЈЁнҠё л””л үнҶ лҰ¬, key(keyrings(7)), к¶Ңн•ң(capabilities(7))мқ„ кІ©лҰ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
A process's user and group IDs can be different inside and outside a user namespace.

н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨмқҳ UIDмҷҖ GIDлҠ” мң м Җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ лӮҙл¶ҖмҷҖ мҷёл¶Җм—җм„ң м„ңлЎң лӢӨлҘј мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
In particular, a process can have a normal unprivileged user ID outside a user namespace while at the same time having a user ID of 0 inside the namespace; in other words, the process has full privileges for operations inside the user namespace, but is unprivileged for operations outside the namespace.

нҠ№нһҲ, н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨлҠ” мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ мҷёл¶Җм—җм„ңлҠ” м •мғҒм Ғмқё к¶Ңн•ңмқҙ м—ҶлҠ” UIDлҘј к°Җм§Җл©ҙм„ң лҸҷмӢңм—җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ лӮҙл¶Җм—җм„ңлҠ” 0мқҳ UIDлҘј к°Җм§Ҳ мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. мҰү, н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨлҠ” мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ лӮҙл¶Җмқҳ мһ‘м—…м—җ лҢҖн•ҙм„ңлҠ” м „мІҙ к¶Ңн•ңмқ„ к°Җм§Җм§Җл§Ң л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ мҷёл¶Җмқҳ мһ‘м—…м—җ лҢҖн•ҙм„ңлҠ” к¶Ңн•ңмқҙ м—ҶмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

- н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨлҠ” н•ң мң м Җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨм—җл§Ң мҶҚн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.
- лі„лҸ„мқҳ м„Өм • м—Ҷмқҙ мһҗмӢқ н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨлҘј мғқм„ұн•ҳл©ҙ н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨлҠ” л¶ҖлӘЁ н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨмқҳ мң м Җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨм—җ мҶҚн•ҳкІҢ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.
- мӢұкёҖ мҠӨл Ҳл“ң н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨлҠ” лӢӨлҘё мң м Җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨлЎң мқҙлҸҷн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- мң м Җ л„Өмһ„мҠӨнҺҳмқҙмҠӨ лӮҙл¶ҖмҷҖ мҷёл¶Җмқҳ м—°кІ°м§Җм җм—җ лҢҖн•ң н‘ңнҳ„мқ„ мң„н•ҙ /proc//uid_mapкіј /proc//gid_mapмқҙ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
