title: RegEx for Vim
text:
## Find

- /: м»Өм„ңк°Җ мң„м№ҳн•ң кіі мқҙнӣ„м—җ мЎҙмһ¬н•ҳлҠ” лӢЁм–ҙлҘј м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- ?: м»Өм„ңк°Җ мң„м№ҳн•ң кіі мқҙм „м—җ мЎҙмһ¬н•ҳлҠ” лӢЁм–ҙлҘј м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- n: мқҙм „ лӢЁм–ҙлҘј м°ҫмқҖ кІҪмҡ° к·ё мқҙм „мңјлЎң, лӢӨмқҢ лӢЁм–ҙлҘј м°ҫмқҖ кІҪмҡ° к·ё лӢӨмқҢмңјлЎң лӢЁм–ҙлҘј м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- N: nкіј л°ҳлҢҖ л°©н–ҘмңјлЎң лӢӨмқҢ лӢЁм–ҙлҘј м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- *: м»Өм„ңк°Җ мң„м№ҳн•ң кіімқҳ лӢЁм–ҙлҘј / лӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
- #: м»Өм„ңк°Җ мң„м№ҳн•ң кіімқҳ лӢЁм–ҙлҘј ? лӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

#кіј *мқҖ нӮӨліҙл“ңм—җм„ң 5, 6мқ„ кё°мӨҖмңјлЎң лҢҖм№ӯмқё мһҗлҰ¬м—җ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

## Substitute

:[]s///[] : beforeм—җм„ң afterлЎң м°ҫм•„ л°”кҝүлӢҲлӢӨ.

- []
 - line optionмқҙ м—ҶлҠ” кІҪмҡ° м»Өм„ңк°Җ мң„м№ҳн•ң мӨ„м—җ м Ғмҡ©лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.
 - start_num,end_num: мЈјм–ҙм§„ лІҲнҳёмқҳ мӨ„мқ„ нҸ¬н•Ён•ң к·ё мӮ¬мқҙ мӨ„
 - \<,\>: Visual лӘЁл“ңм—җм„ң лё”лЎқлҗң мҳҒм—ӯмқҳ мӨ„
 - .,$: нҳ„мһ¬ мң„м№ҳм—җм„ң лҒқк№Ңм§Җ
 - .,+n: нҳ„мһ¬ мң„м№ҳм—җм„ң nлІҲм§ё лӢӨмқҢ мӨ„к№Ңм§Җ
- []
 - g: мӨ„ м „мІҙм—җм„ң м°ҫм•„ л°”кҝүлӢҲлӢӨ. кё°ліёмқҖ мӨ„м—җм„ң м°ҫмқҖ мІ«лІҲм§ёл§Ң л°”кҫёлҠ” кІғмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.(global)
 - c: м°ҫмқҖ нӣ„ л°”кҝҖ кІғмқём§Җ л¬»мҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.(confirmation)
 - i: лҢҖмҶҢл¬ёмһҗлҘј л¬ҙмӢңн•ҳкі  м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. (ignoring case)

## RegEx

- . : мһ„мқҳмқҳ н•ң кёҖмһҗ
- ^x : л¬ёмһҘмқҙ xлЎң мӢңмһ‘
- x$ : л¬ёмһҘмқҙ xлЎң лҒқ
- x* : xк°Җ 0 лІҲ мқҙмғҒ л°ҳліө
- x\+ : xк°Җ 1 лІҲ мқҙмғҒ л°ҳліө
- x\{n,m} : xк°Җ nлІҲ мқҙмғҒ mлІҲ мқҙн•ҳ
 - x\{n} : xк°Җ nлІҲ л°ҳліө
 - x\{n,} : xк°Җ nлІҲ мқҙмғҒ л°ҳліө (к°ҖлҠҘн•ң л§Һмқҙ)
 - x\{,m} : mлІҲ мқҙн•ҳ л°ҳліө (к°ҖлҠҘн•ң л§Һмқҙ)
 - x\{-n,m} : nлІҲ мқҙмғҒ mлІҲ мқҙн•ҳ (к°ҖлҠҘн•ң м ҒкІҢ)
 - x\{-n,} : nлІҲ мқҙмғҒ (к°ҖлҠҘн•ң м ҒкІҢ)
 - x\{-,m} : mлІҲ мқҙн•ҳ (к°ҖлҠҘн•ң м ҒкІҢ)
 - x\{-} : 0лІҲ мқҙмғҒ (к°ҖлҠҘн•ң м ҒкІҢ)
- x\? : xк°Җ мһҲм–ҙлҸ„ лҗҳкі  м—Ҷм–ҙлҸ„ лҗЁ
- x\|y : x лҳҗлҠ” y
- \(\) : к·ёлЈ№
- \ : escape
- [ ] : л¬ёмһҗ нҒҙлһҳмҠӨ
 - [a-z] : a ~ z мӨ‘ н•ң кёҖмһҗ
 - [A-Z] : A ~ Z мӨ‘ н•ң кёҖмһҗ
 - [0-9] : 0 ~ 9 мӨ‘ н•ң мҲ«мһҗ
 - [г„ұ-г…Һ|г…Ҹ-г…Ј|к°Җ-нһЈ] : н•ңкёҖ мӨ‘ н•ң кёҖмһҗ
- \0, & : м°ҫмқҖ л¬ёмһҗ м „мІҙ
- \n : n лІҲм§ё к·ёлЈ№(\(\))

## Examples

- :%s/^\* / \* /g : \*лЎң мӢңмһ‘н•ҳлҠ” мӨ„ 2 м№ё л“Өм—¬м“°кё°
- :%s//`\1/g: \ -> \\\xxxx
- :%s/.\{-}/\1/g` : \web\->web
snippet:
вҖҰ- c: м°ҫмқҖ нӣ„ л°”кҝҖ кІғмқём§Җ л¬»мҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.(confirmation)
 - i: лҢҖмҶҢл¬ёмһҗлҘј л¬ҙмӢңн•ҳкі  м°ҫмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. (ignoring case)

## RegEx

- . : мһ„мқҳмқҳ н•ң кёҖмһҗ
- ^x : л¬ёмһҘмқҙ xлЎң мӢңмһ‘
- x$ : л¬ёмһҘмқҙ xлЎң лҒқ
- x* : xк°Җ 0 лІҲ мқҙмғҒ л°ҳліө
- x\+ : xк°ҖвҖҰ

мң лӢӣмқ„ л“ұлЎқн• л•Ң л“ұлЎқм—җ н•„мҡ”н•ң мң лӢӣ
text:
## Systemd Service

мӢңмһ‘ м„ңл№„мҠӨ л“ұлЎқмқ„ мң„н•ҙ н•„мҡ” мҳөм…ҳл§Ң кіЁлқјм„ң мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©ҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ. лӢӨм–‘н•ң мҳөм…ҳмқҙ мһҲмңјлҜҖлЎң н•„мҡ”м—җ л”°лқј кІҖмғүн•ҙлҙҗм•јн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

``shell
sudo vim /etc/systemd/system/.service
`

`shell
sudo chmod +x /etc/systemd/system/.service
`

### \.service

`makefile
[Unit]
Description=

# мқҳмЎҙ
Requires=

# м•Ҫн•ң мқҳмЎҙ
Wants=

# мһ‘м„ұ мӨ‘мқё м„ңл№„мҠӨ A before B
# B мқҙм „м—җ A мӢӨн–ү
Before=

# мһ‘м„ұ мӨ‘мқё м„ңл№„мҠӨ A after C
# C мқҙнӣ„м—җ A мӢӨн–ү
After=

# C -> A -> B мҲңмңјлЎң мӢӨн–ү лҗЁ

[Service]
User=
Group=

# мӢӨн–ү мң„м№ҳ
WorkingDirectory=

# нҷҳкІҪ ліҖмҲҳ
# нҢҢмқҙмҚ¬ к°ҖмғҒнҷҳкІҪ мӮ¬мҡ© мӢң "PATH=к°ҖмғҒнҷҳкІҪ/bin" м¶”к°Җ
# м—¬лҹ¬лІҲ м„ м–ё к°ҖлҠҘ
Environment=

# м—¬лҹ¬лІҲ м„ м–ё к°ҖлҠҘ
ExecStart=
ExecStop=
Restart=[no|on-success|on-failure|on-watchdog|on-abort|always]

[Install]
# мң лӢӣмқ„ л“ұлЎқн• л•Ң л“ұлЎқм—җ н•„мҡ”н•ң мң лӢӣ
WantedBy=default.target
RequiredBy=
``

text:
- target: Odroid-N2
- tag: v2019.07

## Tips to add/remove boards

``
When adding a new board, the following steps are generally needed:

 [1] Add a header file include/configs/.h
 [2] Make sure to define necessary CONFIG_SYS_* in Kconfig:
 Define CONFIG_SYS_CPU="cpu" to compile arch//cpu/
 Define CONFIG_SYS_SOC="soc" to compile arch//cpu//
 Define CONFIG_SYS_VENDOR="vendor" to compile board//common/*
 and board///*
 Define CONFIG_SYS_BOARD="board" to compile board//*
 (or board///* if CONFIG_SYS_VENDOR is defined)
 Define CONFIG_SYS_CONFIG_NAME="target" to include
 include/configs/.h
 [3] Add a new entry to the board select menu in Kconfig.
 The board select menu is located in arch//Kconfig or
 arch//*/Kconfig.
 [4] Add a MAINTAINERS file
 It is generally placed at board//MAINTAINERS or
 board///MAINTAINERS
 [5] Add configs/_defconfig

When removing an obsolete board, the following steps are generally needed:

 [1] Remove configs/_defconfig
 [2] Remove include/configs/.h if it is not used by any other boards
 [3] Remove board///* or board//* if it is not used
 by any other boards
 [4] Update MAINTAINERS if necessary
 [5] Remove the unused entry from the board select menu in Kconfig
 [6] Add an entry to doc/README.scrapyard
`

Configuration files for use in C sources

- include/generated/autoconf.h (generated by Kconfig for Normal)
- include/configs/.h (exists for all boards)

Configuration file for use in makefiles

- include/config/auto.conf (generated by Kconfig)
- include/autoconf.mk (generated by the old config for Normal)
- spl/include/autoconfig.mk (generated by the old config for SPL)
- tpl/include/autoconfig.mk (generated by the old config for TPL)
When adding a new CONFIG macro, it is highly recommended to add it to Kconfig
rather than to a header file.

title: Neural Network CPU vs. GPU vs. NPU
description: Neural Network CPU vs. GPU vs. NPU
text:
## Google's product News

### How a CPU works

...
A CPU has to store the calculation results on memory inside CPU (so called registers or L1 cache) for every single calculation. This memory access becomes the downside of CPU architecture called the **von Neumann bottleneck**.
...

### How a GPU works

...
The modern GPU usually has 2,500вҖ“5,000 ALUs in a single processor that means you could execute thousands of multiplications and additions simultaneously. For every single calculation in the thousands of ALUs, GPU need to access registers or shared memory to read and store the intermediate calculation results.
...

### How a TPU works

...
The key enabler is a major reduction of the von Neumann bottleneck. So hardware designer place thousands of multipliers and adders and connect them to each other directly to form a large physical matrix of those operators. This is called **systolic array** architecture.

Let's see how a systolic array executes the neural network calculations. At first, TPU loads the parameters from memory into the matrix of multipliers and adders.

Then, the TPU loads data from memory. As each multiplication is executed, the result will be passed to next multipliers while taking summation at the same time. So the output will be the summation of all multiplication result between data and parameters. During the whole process of massive calculations and data passing, **no memory access is required at all**.
...

### CNS-core

### N-core

title: Derivative
text:
## Loss function

### Mean Square Error

$$
Loss = \dfrac{ 1 }{ 2 } \sum_i \left( y_i - a^L_i \right)^2
$$

$$
\dfrac{ \partial Loss }{ \partial a^L_i } = - \left( y_i - a^L_i \right)
$$

### Binary Cross-Entropy

$$
Loss = \sum_i \left[ -y_i \log a^L_i - (1 - y_i) \log \left( 1 - a^L_i \right) \right]
$$

$$
\dfrac{\partial Loss}{\partial a^L_i}
= \dfrac{ - \left( y_i - a^L_i \right)}{a^L_i \left( 1 - a^L_i \right) }
$$

## Activation

### logistic

$$
a^l_i = \sigma ( z^l_i ) = logistic ( z^l_i ) = \dfrac{ 1 }{ 1 + e^{ -z^l_i } }
$$

$$
\dfrac{\partial \sigma ( z^l_i )}{\partial z^l_i }
= \dfrac{ e^{ -z^l_i } }{ \left( 1 + e^{ -z^l_i } \right)^2 }
= a^l_i \left( 1 - a^l_i \right)
$$
